dishes.description: Seared blackening-spiced tuna wrapped in a 12-inch flour tortilla with rice, cheese, garlic, tomatoes, veggie puree, mushrooms, and a creamy honey wasabi sauce, then baked.

I'll do that all day long. The honey wasabi sauce is absolutely what ties it all together. Just enough heat, but then you get the back side of the sweet right at the same time. Good job, man.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 9 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So here we are in Nolansville to check out a joint where it's all about the old school whole Hog low and slow barbecue. This is Martin's Barbecue Joint.

dishes.description: 200+ pound hog rubbed with 'big hoss rub' (brown sugar, garlic, paprika, chili powder, cayenne, lemon pepper, onion powder, dry mustard, cinnamon, ginger, black pepper), smoked for 26 hours, and basted with a Carolina-style vinegar-based sauce. Served with various sauces including Beach Road 12, Devil's Nectar, and Sweet Dixie.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, the pork is beyond juicy and moist and flavorful. Not over-smoked. Yeah. Little sweet, little spice, not too much vinegar. All the red from the chili. Got a little more sugar. Still got some of that tang. I'll take two gallons of that. Give me 14 pounds of this. It's prime time, man.

dishes.description: Pulled pork (smoked for 20 hours with big hoss rub) served on a cornbread pancake (made with buttermilk, egg, pepper, pork cooking liquid), topped with coleslaw and Sweet Dixie sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. That's good. It's like breakfast pulled pork. I just got to give you the respect first, but the slaw, nice crunch, nice vinegar. The Dixie sauce, you get the sweet and the deep-rich flavor of the pork. It's got a lot of balance. This cornmeal cake, nice crust on that. Great idea. Totally ingenious.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 9 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: What does Cincinnati have? That's right, Cincinnati-style chili. Now, Cincinnatians go through more than 2 million pounds of chili a year, topped with over 850,000 pounds of cheese. Now, there's been a big debate. Who made it first and who makes it best? Well, everybody has their opinion, but a lot of you wrote in and sent me to a place that I'd never even heard of, Blue Ash Chili, 15 miles north of Cincinnati. And my only question now is, am I going to have it two-way, four-way, five-way, or at this joint, six-way?

dishes.description: Cincinnati-style chili (ground beef, onions, tomato paste, paprika, cumin, cinnamon, cracker meal) served over spaghetti, topped with onions, kidney beans, cheese, and beer-battered jalapeno bottle caps.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm! The chili has a unique spice to it. The onions are nice and fresh, and the pasta really works. Cooked al dente, which I think is critical, having the beans in there, there's a texture that you expect to see in chili, but the beans are a little bit more whole. Right. Then you put a ridiculous amount of cheese on it. That's a great way to do chili. Mmm! I could eat that six or seven times a week.

dishes.description: Homemade roasted top round beef, sliced thin, served on a toasted bun with sauteed green/red peppers, onions, and Jack Daniel's whiskey sauteed mushrooms, provolone cheese, then smothered with Cincinnati chili.
dishes.guy_response: Tasty. Nice toasted bun. Fresh veggies in there. The chili. It's ridiculous. You're showing Cincinnati chili can go on just about anything.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 9 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Well, I hear if you want the bomb Hawaiian food drive-in right outside of Waikiki, this is the joint. Yeah. 48 years of serving up Hawaiian specialties like shoyu chicken and loco moco. That's why I'm waiting in line here at the Rainbow Drive-In.

dishes.description: Two hamburger patties served over rice, topped with a rich gravy (made from chicken stock, dried shrimp, ginger, kombu, natto puree, garlic, tomato puree, Worcestershire, chili powder, and flour slurry) and a fried egg.
dishes.guy_response: That's great gravy. Thank you. And the patty's perfect. It's nice and tender. A little bit of onion in there. I think if people look at it and say, I've heard of loco moco. I don't know. Come try it here. That gravy will make a believer out of you. Dried shrimp. What just happened to me? The gravy is to die for.

dishes.description: Two hot dogs served over rice and macaroni salad, topped with a hearty chili (ground beef, tomatoes, onions, kidney beans, tomato puree, salt, black pepper, cayenne, cumin, Worcestershire, garlic, chili powder, and flour slurry).
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Might have something there, buddy. So you get the cool and the creamy with the spicy and meaty. Spicy and meaty. Mmm. I'll tell you, that rice just plays in so nicely. The rice is so nice. Chili is dynamite. Mixing it with the mac salad, right on point. Mmm. Nice job.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 9 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So here we are in the heart of Thoroughbred country, Midway, Kentucky. Not to see the Derby winners. Nope. To see what's cooking. Wallace Station. Burgers are ready to pick up. It's a little slice of heaven. They're looking good. They really went out of their way building these burgers and these sandwiches.

dishes.description: Local bison burger seasoned with 'chicken salt' (kosher salt, black pepper, paprika, cayenne), grilled, topped with provolone cheese, ham, bacon, tomato, and a creamy Mornay sauce (butter, flour, milk, kosher salt, cayenne, Vermont cheddar).
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Mmm. That to me rivals with the traditional aspects of the hot brown. I think I might like this better. Nice beef. Great bun. Not too much Mornay. You're not losing the other flavors. I'm not kidding you. I'm gonna take one more bite. I don't want to do this, but I'm doing this for you.

dishes.dish_name: Country Ham and Pimento Cheese Sandwich
dishes.description: Country ham, homemade pimento cheese (shredded white cheddar, cream cheese, mayonnaise, pimentos, hot sauce, Worcestershire, pureed onion), and tomato, grilled on fresh country white bread.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Really good. Got really good chew. Now I'm gonna tell you something. You don't find pimento cheese if people are making themselves. That right there has all the flavors and to know that we just made the bread. Mmm. Mmm, that's good. You don't find the fresh homemade stuff very often.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 9 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So here we are. Triple D Kansas City barbecue tour in Mission, Kansas. Now check out this story. A local dude packs up, moves to New York City to get his culinary creds in a French restaurant and spend 25 years in the high end joints. Now in the meantime he's moonlighting in competition barbecue till he realizes this is what I love. It's time we check out RJ's barbecue.

dishes.description: Ground pork butt mixed with nutmeg, fresh garlic, salt, cayenne pepper, jalapenos, sugar, and red pepper, wrapped in corn husks and smoked for an hour and a half.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. It's super tender. It did get the smoke. It went long enough that the jalapenos mellowed out. I'm going to politely tell you that I will be ripping off the corn husk sausage idea. Jalapeno sausage. It's a great combination, the sausage and the jalapeno. It's tremendous. It just crumbles in your mouth.

dishes.description: Denver lamb ribs seasoned with a complex rub including corn dextrose, basil, chili powder, cumin, brown sugar, rosemary, and mustard flour, then smoked for two and a half hours at 250 degrees with the silver skin on.
dishes.guy_response: It's sweet. Oh, man. Tender. Mmm. The rub doesn't overpower it. Sits there and just kind of supports it. You still get to taste the lamb. Mmm. Mmm. Nice smoke ring. I think leaving that silver skin on, which I'm not a big fan of on baby backs, but on this, I really think it did lock in the moisture. Great stuff.

visits.guy_intro: Top-notch burger joint that is all about simple, fast, and fresh. This is Burger Me.

dishes.guy_response: Mmm. That's an all-American burger. The tomato's nice, pickle's in there, burger's cooked the right way. Don't pick up too much of the peanut butter. That would be one I would definitely order again. Mmm. Nice job.

dishes.description: Traditional white bread, made by eyeballing ingredients, lightly kneaded, proofed, and baked until golden.

dishes.description: Chicken marinated for at least a day in garlic, salt, basil, oregano, parsley, oil, and lemon juice, then grilled and served on a salad with iceberg lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, kalamata olives, feta, red onion, and a homemade dressing.

It's not a bite you see every day on Triple D, right? Nice big salad shot. Really, really good, man. I'm not going to lie, but it's chicken souvlaki. I love it. I love the chicken. Extraordinary. It's really good.

dishes.description: Tonkatsu broth with char siu pork belly, noodles, corn, pickled daikon radish, and soy-marinated eggs, topped with melted raclette cheese. The broth is enriched with charred garlic, ginger, onions, chicken and pig's feet, and cream cheese.

And it works in tandem with the funk of the raclette. I can really taste the caramelization on the pork. It's not supposed to work. But once you have one bite of it, you're like, of course that works. It's completely crazy, but yeah, it's great.

dishes.guy_response: Mmm. What do you think? It's rich. It's flaky. It is outstanding. Yeah, whitefish has a really clean flavor. Mmm. So balanced. It's not sweet. It's not salty. It's not over-smoked. It's just got this really nice, easy-going flavor.

A nice blend between cheese and sauce, a little bit of spice from the vinegar of the sweet peppers, creaminess of the garlic, wow. It's not Thanksgiving turkey that someone carved from the table. I mean, real deal, man. Real deal. If you're going to have a gourmet burger joint, you better serve up a gourmet fry. That's it, right? Excellent.

I understand why you serve the claw with it. But you know what? I think for people that really like martinis and like them dirty, that's got it. It's absolutely lobster martini. Are we just pinching them off on the side like this? Absolutely. Oven. 350, about 30 minutes. We brush them at the very end with melted butter. What are we making next? Red pepper jelly.

dishes.description: Savory cheesecake with a Creole-seasoned breadcrumb crust, cream cheese, smoked gouda, Fontina, Romano, Parmesan, sauteed onions, garlic, green onions, lobster base, lemon juice, white pepper, and Louisiana crawfish tails. Served with Creole Meuniere sauce and hollandaise.
dishes.guy_response: It's delicious. That is so rich. And there is so much flavor. It's silky, great amount of cheese, perfectly seasoned. Good amount of the crawfish inside there. You're actually really happy when you get that little nugget. The sauces complement it incredibly well. The crust plays a pivotal role. That's my kind of cheesecake. I've got a crawfish cheesecake right here, baby.

dishes.description: Tongue sliced, seasoned with kosher salt and granulated garlic, breaded in flour and egg, fried, then simmered in a sauce with olive oil, crushed red pepper, onion, garlic puree, churroseta pepper sauce, salt, paprika, and tomato sauce.
dishes.guy_response: It's fantastic. The tongue, that is tender. It's like roast beef. The paprika, tomato, garlic sauce is off the chain. Yum, yum. Really good sauce.

dishes.description: Breaded lamb testicles.
Pig ears seasoned with sugar, salt, cumin, paprika, chili powder, garlic, then slow-cooked in water and vinegar for about eight hours. Served on a sandwich with mustard and chopped onion.
dishes.guy_response: It's tender. It is tender. See the cartilage? Cooked down to a nice, tender state. It is very tasty. I would call it natural bologna. I didn't really think I was going to be too big of a fan. Once you're here, you love it.

dishes.description: Ground alligator meat (solo cut) formed into patties, seasoned with salt, pepper, paprika, and garlic powder, then grilled. Served with a gravy made from water, dried bananas, dried fruit (apples, apricots, plums), baby shrimp, and curry, finished with cream.
dishes.guy_response: I gotta tell you, I never thought I would really like it. Look at that. That's awesome. Great flavor to it. And the curry fruit thing, you know what? That's crazy. Actually tastes like a pork cutlet. It seems like all the flavors meld together to make one very fine burger.

dishes.description: Pizza with a high-gluten flour starter dough, topped with French soubise, Gruyere cheese, roasted carrots (seasoned with salt, pepper, orange zest, honey, butter, orange juice), and Fresno pepper. Garnished with Dukkah (Moroccan spice blend of almonds, sesame seeds, cumin, hazelnuts, black peppercorn, coriander, fennel, salt, pepper, paprika). Cooked in a wood-fired oven.
dishes.guy_response: Outstanding. The tang of the gruyere. The sweetness of the caramelized carrot. The spice of the Fresno. Without the duca, you sprinkle on, like, pixie dust. Wouldn't be a second. It's giving me goosebumps. That is probably in the top three most unique pizzas I've ever had in my life.

dishes.description: Boneless beef short ribs seasoned with salt and black pepper, seared, then braised in a hotel pan with mirepoix (carrot, celery, onion), fresh thyme, bay leaves, chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, homemade chicken stock, and Dr. Pepper for four hours at 350 degrees. Served with celery root puree, sautéed mustard greens, and fresh grated horseradish.
dishes.guy_response: The short rib is so tender that it's just falling apart. You just have to help it with the fork a little bit. The celery root puree is a great medium, the whole dish. The fresh horseradish, along with the mustard greens, adds a nice contrast to this braising liquid that's been reduced. And the Dr. Pepper, just this nice, round flavor, and mm, every little piece you did here, killer. I mean, really killer.

dishes.description: Duck tongues and duck neck seasoned with salt, onion, hot sauce, cilantro, peppercorns, garlic, and tortilla paste, then cooked in the oven for 3.5 hours at 300 degrees. Meat is shredded, cartilage removed, then caramelized with scallions and cilantro. Served in taco shells with chopped onions and queso fresco.
dishes.guy_response: Delicious. There's a depth of flavor that's coming through, one, because of this fantastic marinade that you made, two, with the richness of cooking something like the neck. I know it's a little tongue-in-cheek. Now that one, you've been sitting on it for a while. It's creative. The texture is great. The crispiness that you generate from that meat, to the tenderness that you get from the tongue, the acidic contrast that you get from the pickled onions, and then the little crunchy taco with it, that's some dynamite duck.

dishes.description: Pizza with shredded beef marinated in Italian dressing, cola, soy sauce, granulated garlic, pepper, and salt. Topped with provolone, mozzarella, the marinated steak (raw), gorgonzola, and caramelized onions. Baked in the oven for eight minutes at 550 degrees.
dishes.guy_response: Right balance of cheese, and meat, and sauce. Not soggy. Bro, you got a pizza here. It's meaty, it's rich, and delivered on a crust that is up there with some of the best I've had. Chewy, yet tender. It's not overdone with the ingredients. The attention to detail is really paying off. Because I thought, it's either going to be a hero or a zero. That's a hero, that's a dynamite pie.

dishes.description: St. Louis-style pork spare ribs soaked in lemon juice and vinegar for a day, seasoned with salt and pepper, then coated in a house seasoning (flour, salt, cornstarch, baking powder, pepper) and deep-fried for 15 minutes. Served with a homemade barbecue sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Not too much batter. Nice and juicy. I think the key was that you soaked them in the vinegar. Got a nice little flavor from that. How do you like that sauce? Oh, there's good tang in that sauce, ma'am. It's already a good rib, but when you put that batter on top of it, it just takes it over the top.

dishes.description: Made with AP flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, butter, and buttermilk, pinched off and baked at 350 degrees for 30 minutes, brushed with melted butter at the end.
dishes.guy_response: Biscuit's spot on.

dishes.description: Made with roasted red peppers, sugar, apple cider vinegar, butter, and pectin, brought to a boil and cooled.
dishes.guy_response: Little jelly's beautiful.

dishes.description: Chicken brined for two days in a sweet tea brine (sugar, salt, hot water, garlic, black pepper, lemons, bay leaves, parsley, iced tea), then battered in day-old pancake batter and a flour dredge, cooked low and slow.
dishes.guy_response: But boy, the crunch on that chicken. That batter, really tasty.

dishes.description: A biscuit topped with country sausage gravy (oil blend, butter, onions, chorizo, country sausage mix, cayenne, sage, heavy cream, culinary cream, whole milk, green onion, black pepper, kosher salt, dried sage), applewood smoked bacon, and fried chicken (buttermilk, hot sauce, salt, pepper, AP flour, coarse ground pepper, white pepper, garlic powder, canola oil, topped with pepper jack and mustard cheese, broiled).
dishes.guy_response: I can't say I'm that much of a biscuit aficionado. But this is nice and light and fluffy. Nice crust on top. Here we go. So the gravy is not really big and heavy. It's a lot lighter than I thought it would be. When you come to North Carolina and you think that you're going to have a gravy, bacon, chicken, cheese, biscuit meal, that's the kind of dish you fall asleep in.

dishes.description: House-cut, twice-fried red potatoes topped with slow-cooked bison osso bucco, green chili gravy, and melted Gruyère cheese.
dishes.guy_response: The gruyere, I can smell it right off the bat. That's remarkable. I mean, like, this is like getting a bite of your favorite steak dinner. Big, rich beef flavor coming from that osso bucco. The fry has tremendous potato flavor. It's just submersed in enough of that jus. The gruyere got its way to melt down, nook and cranny, all the way to the bottom of the fries. That's a destination dish. I'd come back tomorrow for this and the next day. All right, bison gravy dirty fries.

visits.video_title: Wait, They Put WHAT In That?! Top 10 Times Guy Fieri Was Shocked on #DDD 😱 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Brains and eggs. Cap brains and eggs, is that the specialty? It's just what we eat for breakfast every morning here at Cattleman's.

dishes.description: Brains cooked with scrambled eggs and steak seasoning.
dishes.guy_response: I don't like eggs, and I don't like brains. I definitely don't like egg and brains. Oh, god. I'm going to need toast. So the yellow part's the chicken, and the brown part is the brain matter. That's correct. You going to make me smarter? It's guaranteed to make you smarter. Can I have some sauce or something? There you go. Mind over matter, right? God, I don't want to do it!

visits.video_title: Guy Devours LEGENDARY Pizza Loaf & Sausage in Portland | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: In 2013, I was on the southeast side of Portland, Oregon to check out a market cranking out German sausage. This is Edelweiss Sausage and Delicatessen. Started by George Baer, who brought all his meatpacking skills from Berlin and opened this place in 82. Then he passed that knowledge on to his sons, Tom and Tony.

dishes.description: Salami made from frozen pork fat and beef bottom rounds, seasoned with curing salt, sugar, dextrose, white pepper, ground and whole fennel, juniper berry, crushed garlic, and Italian red wine. Stuffed and cured for a couple of days.
dishes.guy_response: I would do damage on a deli with this.

dishes.description: Cow top round sliced over a quarter inch, marinated with salt, brown sugar, black pepper, curing salt, clover honey, and a spicy Thai chili pepper seasoning blend (Thai chilies, fresh garlic, rice wine vinegar, brown sugar). Tumbled for four hours, seasoned with crushed red pepper and black pepper, then smoked at 200 degrees for two hours.
dishes.guy_response: There's a pretty good balance between the spiciness and the sweetness. And on top of that, the bite is really nice. It's so tender. If you like a kick in your beef jerky, this is definitely the jerky for you.

dishes.description: An emulsion of diced back fat, ground chuck and pork shoulder, kept cold with ice. Seasoned with salt, sugar, cure, dried mustard, smoked paprika, mace, cumin, cardamom, coriander, white pepper, and garlic powder. Mixed with sun-dried tomatoes, crushed red pepper, toasted onions, fire-roasted bell peppers, Swiss cheese, cheddar, and black olives. Baked in a loaf pan at 250 degrees for two and a half hours. Sliced, grilled, chopped into sticks, and served with homemade marinara.
dishes.guy_response: The pizza loaf is phenomenal. It's got a little crunch on the outside. It reminds me a little bit of mozzarella sticks, but meatier. It has such a nice flavor to it. It's really delicious.

name: Intermezzo Pizzeria
owner_chef: Georgie Avramovic, Bronco Avramovic and Dobrila Avramovic

visits.video_title: Guy Devours LEGENDARY Pizza Loaf & Sausage in Portland | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I heard the term living the American dream while I'm here in Charlotte, North Carolina, a mile from the downtown area where a Serbian family is living the American dream. They got a funky little joint where they're serving up. Now get this, pizza and Serbian food. This is intermezzo pizzeria.

visits.video_title: Top 20 Craziest Pizzas on #DDD w/ Guy Fieri (Extended) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: You know, we've all heard the term, live in the American dream. Well, I'm here in Charlotte, North Carolina, a mile from the downtown area. We're a Serbian family. Our family is living the American dream. They got a funky little joint where they're serving up, now get this, pizza and Serbian food, just what you'd expect. This is Intermezzo Pizzeria.

dishes.description: A calzone-like dish with dough filled with mozzarella cheese, ricotta, parsley, garlic, mushrooms, and ham. Baked at 505 degrees for 10 minutes. Served with sour cream, provolone cheese, and pizza sauce on top.
dishes.guy_response: Really? Really good. The yeast in the dough really adds a nice tang. The sour cream, without question, is fantastic. And then the mushrooms and the ham, they're dynamite. I'm actually disappointed that I haven't tried this before. Pirozka. Outstanding. It's like the flavor explosion of pizza that's inside out. One of the best things I've ever eaten.

dishes.description: A calzone-like dish with pizza dough filled with mozzarella, ricotta, parsley, garlic, mushrooms, and ham. Cooked at 505F and served with sour cream, provolone cheese, and house-made pizza sauce on top.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, that's really, really good. The yeast in the dough really adds a nice tang. The sour cream, without question, is fantastic. And then the mushrooms and the ham, they're dynamite. I'm actually disappointed that I haven't tried this before. You learn something new every day. Pirozhka. Pirozhka. Outstanding.

visits.video_title: Guy Devours LEGENDARY Pizza Loaf & Sausage in Portland | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Hunter and I are right off the strip in Las Vegas. This is Marsigliano's Pizzeria and More. Richie Marsigliano walked away from his family's pizza business as a kid on Staten Island, only to come full circle in Vegas after a tough life lesson.

visits.video_title: 10 Craziest #DDD Lasagna Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So Hunter and I are right off the strip in Las Vegas. And Hunter, when you were going to UNLV, what was one of your favorite places to eat at? ... This is Marsigliano's Pizzeria and More.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Las Vegas | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So Hunter and I are right off the strip in Las Vegas. This is Marsigliano's Pizzeria and More.

dishes.description: Dough made with water, yeast, onion powder, sugar, and flour, proofed and rested overnight. Marinara sauce made with browned garlic, blended celery and onions, red and green bell pepper, red wine, tomatoes, parsley, granulated garlic, black pepper, oregano, onion powder, sugar, and fresh basil. Chicken breast is fileted, pounded, dredged in AP flour, egg wash, and Italian breadcrumbs, then fried. Diced chicken, mozzarella, ricotta, and marinara sauce are wrapped in pizza dough, baked at 500 degrees for about eight minutes. Dressed with garlic butter, Parmesan cheese, and parsley.
dishes.guy_response: Great crust. The onion, it sings all the way through. Gives you that little kick. It's very hearty. And the marinara, nice, fresh, clean. I think it's dynamite.

dishes.description: A unique pizza combining traditional lasagna and pizza, featuring bolognese sauce, marinara, mozzarella, ricotta, and a second crust on top, finished with garlic butter and parmesan.
dishes.guy_response: Oh my gosh. It's got great texture. It's got great chew. I think it's a genius. I see it. Huh? You're a madman. Man's an animal. Pizza lasagna ready to go. Top it off. The top layer is kind of crispy with that garlicy flavor. Right? Pizza and lasagna in the same dish? It's amazing.

visits.video_title: Guy Devours LEGENDARY Pizza Loaf & Sausage in Portland | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: The next time you find yourself in Robbinsville, New Jersey, you got to check out Dolce & Clemente. With your Italian specialties, owner Joe Clemente has learned over a lifetime. He and Frank joined forces to open up this mother of a market deli and restaurant.

dishes.description: Dough made with water, yeast, salt, sugar, oil, and high-quality flour, mixed, portioned, and rested overnight. Filled with prosciutto, ham, mortadella, super salvo, pepperoni, provolone cheese, and crushed black pepper. The dough is folded and kneaded, then baked at 350 degrees for 38 minutes. Served with olive oil, locatelli cheese, and parsley.
dishes.guy_response: That is ridiculous. But when you cook these Italian meats and you let all the fat, cook into the dough, you just get all the flavor. The depth of flavor in this is massive. The key, though, is how you cook the meat on the outside of the bread. Kisses that crunch. Out of bounds. Seriously, out of bounds.

visits.video_title: Guy Devours LEGENDARY Pizza Loaf & Sausage in Portland | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here at Jackson, Wyoming, about a half block off the town square, right in front of the Pink Garter Theater. Locals around here tell me you want some rockin' pizza, you gotta go to the joint right under the theater. This is Pinky G's Pizzeria. These pizzas are the creation of chef and owner Tom Fay, who came back home after culinary school and opened this joint in 2011.

visits.video_title: Top 20 #DDD Videos in the Rockies with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here at Jackson, Wyoming, about a half block off the town square, right in front of the Pink Garter Theater. Now, back in the day, this was the theater that you came to to catch a musical or a play. Now it's where you come to see some rockin' bands. And speaking of rockin', locals around here tell me, you want some rockin' pizza, you got to go to the joint right under the theater. This is Pinky G's Pizzeria.

visits.video_title: Wildest Meat Lover Pizza Videos on #DDD with Guy Fieri 🥩🍕 Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here at Jackson, Wyoming, about a half block off the town square, right in front of the Pink Garter Theater. Now, back in the day, this was the theater that you came to to catch a musical or a play. Now it's where you come to see some rockin' bands. And speaking of rockin', locals around here tell me, you want some rockin' pizza? Go to the joint right under the theater. This is Pinky G's Pizzeria.

dishes.description: Chicken breast marinated for 24 hours with fresh chopped basil, garlic, lemon slices, and oil, then roasted at 575 degrees for 25 minutes. Hand-tossed pizza dough (cornmeal and flour) topped with fresh basil pesto, mozzarella cheese, roasted chicken, artichoke hearts, red onions, and ricotta cheese. Baked at 575 degrees for 10 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: Roasting the chicken with the garlic, outrageous. Making your own pesto over the top. Little bit of that ricotta coming in. Creaminess, saltiness.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Southern BBQ Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

dishes.description: Brisket ground with chuck, seasoned, grilled, served on toasted bun with Spice Carolina sauce, cheddar cheese, rib candy, peppered bacon, pickles, tomato, onion, and pimento cheese patty.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, that's a great burger. It's super tender brisket. When you chop that brisket up, you get a chance to incorporate that fat. So it makes it even a little bit more unctuous. And adding the pimento cheese and adding the veggies on top of it brings that burger element to it. That's Fox Brothers right there. Damn, man. Come on. That is just getting it done.

visits.video_title: 5 Craziest #DDD Ribs Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I've had a lot of alligator tail, but nothing compares to the alligator rib here. Alligator tail is kind of like chicken, where this actually seems to be like a pork.

dishes.description: Alligator ribs with a dry rub of brown sugar, chili, garlic powder, onion powder, cinnamon, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper, smoked for 40 minutes, then tossed in a homemade barbecue sauce and grilled for 5-10 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: That's probably one of the best ways I've ever had gator. If not the best way. It's tender. It's meaty. It eats like a pork rib. Right. The barbecue sauce, dude. I compete in barbecue. I'm in the Barbecue Hall of Fame. The barbecue sauce is delicious.

visits.video_title: 5 Craziest #DDD Ribs Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: All right, brother. We're into our beef ribs. Bone-in chuck ribs. Holy moly. Yeah, that's one rack.

dishes.description: Bone-in chuck ribs, trimmed of fat, seasoned with Mama's rub, and smoked for about five hours.
dishes.guy_response: That's no joke. Tender, not dried out. That is a righteous rib. That's got its own voodoo going on right there. You look at the smoke ring on that beef rib, and you look at the size of it. It's just simple, straightforward good.

dishes.description: English cucumbers and sliced onions salted and iced, then brined in apple cider vinegar, sugar, mustard seed, celery seed, and turmeric.
dishes.guy_response: And the bread and butter pickle, still enough body to it, still enough crunch, good amount of acid, nice little bit of sweetness to it. Like all the onion. Mm, that's a good pickle. That's a real good pickle.

dishes.description: Homemade mustard made with apple cider vinegar, egg yolk, dry mustard, sugar, and standard yellow mustard, cooked in a double boiler.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, that goes great with that rib.

visits.video_title: 5 Craziest #DDD Ribs Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Big Lee's smoked ribs and a Kathy. They're good ribs, because they have meat on them. We love the Kathy. The brisket with the mac and cheese, I mean, you create a little space of heaven.

dishes.description: Spare ribs trimmed and seasoned with Big Lee's house rub (salt, coarse ground black pepper, granulated onion, granulated garlic, oregano, basil, rosemary, thyme), dry-rubbed for 24-48 hours, then smoked low and slow over Central Florida oak until tender. Served with Flame Tang barbecue sauce.
dishes.guy_response: There's more smoke ring than there is meat. Juicy is an understatement. Dynamite bark seasoned all the way through from that dry brine. That barbecue sauce, there's definitely good heat in it, definitely good vinegar in it. Every single thing here has its own uniqueness about it. That's what takes this to the next level.

dishes.dish_name: The Cathy (Brisket Stuffed Mac and Cheese)
dishes.description: Mac and cheese made with a cheese sauce (butter, kosher salt, sugar, processed cheese, extra sharp cheddar, milk) and cooked noodles, layered with chopped sauced brisket and shredded Gouda, then topped with more sharp cheddar and smoked for 30 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: Dynamite. I mean, mac and cheese with a dollop of smoked brisket on top of it would not be the same. There's something about infusing the flavor of that brisket into the mac and cheese.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Meat (beef/lamb) seasoned with garlic powder, basil, oregano, paprika, onion powder, salt, pepper, stacked on a spit, cooked on a rotisserie, shaved thin, served in pita with tzatziki.
dishes.guy_response: Incredible crust, but tender, outstanding. I might like that more than the Italian beef. You will be disappointed if you eat one anywhere else after that.

dishes.description: Beef tenderloin strips marinated in pureed onion juice, pepper, garlic powder, kosher salt, saffron water, tenderized with a knife, skewered, grilled, served with rice pilaf.
dishes.guy_response: The saffron butter, the filet, it is tender, not overcooked. I don't know which one I like better.

dishes.description: Chuck roll ground with onion pulp, salt, black pepper, garlic powder, beef stock, formed onto a one-inch skewer using a machine, grilled, served with rice pilaf.
dishes.guy_response: The way it's compressed gives it a different texture. You can't tell this is ground beef. I don't know which one I like better. The ground meat's a little bit lighter, and it is a lot. But as you can tell, I don't like it.

dishes.description: Layers of fried and baked potatoes, eggplant, and ground meat (beef/lamb) cooked with extra virgin olive oil, onions, garlic, bay leaves, pepper, cinnamon sticks, cloves, fresh tomatoes, tomato puree, topped with bechamel sauce, baked.
dishes.guy_response: It's really delicious. It's sort of a comfort food. Soft and creamy on top. It really blends well with the warm spices underneath.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Lamb and beef mixed with coriander, cumin seed, black peppercorn, currant, onion, roasted garlic, eggs, fresh garlic, cinnamon, red chili flake, fresh parsley, salt, formed into footballs, par-cooked, served with tomato relish, roasted garlic yogurt, tahini sauce, zatar, pomegranate molasses, and fresh pita.
dishes.guy_response: It's dynamite. The kofta is nice and tender. The tomato relish, crispy and bright. Everything is just done right and just fulfilling. The lamb meatballs have so much flavor, and the garlic yogurt. The yogurt sauce is really subtle, but really flavorful. Their flatbread is fresh and puffy.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Pizza dough (like pita dough) with a garlic potato spread, cubed mozzarella, chicken shawarma, artichoke hearts, mushrooms, onions, baked.
dishes.guy_response: The chicken is amazing. I mean, it's got such a big, full flavor. And I love that you kept spinning it, so almost every piece of meat had that caramelization. The cream sauce, you get that little touch of garlic, but the garlic isn't overtaking the pizza. Really nice crust. Outstanding, my friend.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Dough made with flour, eggs, salt, warm water, filled with ground beef mixed with pureed onion, black pepper, paprika, salt, Aleppo pepper, folded into small squares, boiled, served with a tomato garlic sauce (garlic, tomato paste, red pepper paste, dry mint, water, salt), yogurt sauce (yogurt, salt, garlic, water), and rice and noodle pilaf, topped with almonds.
dishes.guy_response: Wow. I love the tang and the brightness that comes from the tomato sauce. Beautiful. I feel the family heritage in this dish. The dumpling is nice and meaty, but the real body of the dish is the rice pilaf. It's really just an amazing dish. I mean, just flavor all the way through your palate. This is the kind of dish that's worth driving an hour across town to come and get.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Phyllo dough layered with a filling of sautéed onion, scallions, dill, black pepper, nutmeg, salt, spinach, feta cheese, and eggs, baked until golden and flaky.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, man. That is excellent. Man, that's great flavor. That's awesome. Flaky, the cheese inside. Absolutely excellent.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Leg of lamb cleaned, cooked in peanut oil with green cardamom, cardamom leaf, flaxseed, dried cardamom, bay leaves, cinnamon sticks, cumin, coriander, turmeric, saffron, Himalayan salt, pepper, garlic, ginger. Served with a sauce made from peanut oil, fennel seed, ajwan, cinnamon sticks, Korean cumin, turmeric, black Syrian cardamom, saffron, ginger, pureed garlic, cardamom leaves, Himalayan salt, bay leaf, tomato puree, sour cream, homemade yogurt, milk, simmered for hours. Garnished with dry cherry, raisins, cilantro, parsley.
dishes.guy_response: Are you kidding me? Fantastic. You are surrounded in flavor. Wow. You probably not have lamb this tender. This is like Mediterranean. This is like Mediterranean. Mediterranean bolognese. I'm sorry, I got to do it one more bite. Outstanding.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Lamb shanks trimmed, marinated in lemon juice, Greek oregano, black pepper, seasoned salt, fresh garlic, salt, Greek olive oil (using a vacuum tumbler for quick penetration), then baked covered for two hours, uncovered and flipped for another hour. Served with roasted potatoes, small Greek salad, pita breads, tzatziki sauce, and lemon.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, that's great. That's a really nice marinade. The lemon juice? Probably one of the more lemon-forward, lamb shanks I've ever had. Mm, you're going to have to take this away. Great.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Tri-tip beef marinated for two days in vinegar, white wine, oil, allspice, seven spice, black pepper, cardamom, salt, onions, bay leaves, lemon juice, stacked on a skewer with fat and onion, cooked on a rotisserie. Served with hummus (garbanzo beans, tahini, salt, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil), onion and sumac salad, parsley, tahini, and bread.
dishes.guy_response: It's really light and fluffy. I feel so warm up from the shawarma. The flavor of the lemon is incredible and how it sort of tenderizes the meat. There's so much flavor in the meat. And when you get that hummus with it, and the pilaf. That's a meal right there. Beautiful. This is the way I want to eat five nights a week. Delicious.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Ground meat (rib eye roll, short plate, chuck) mixed with ground red bell pepper, green bell peppers, white onions, jalapeños, turmeric, paprika, smoked paprika, chili powder, onion powder, black pepper, salt, formed onto skewers, grilled over mesquite. Served with basmati rice (with turmeric, garlic powder, chicken broth, vegetable oil), grilled Roma tomatoes, and a chimichurri sauce (green bell pepper, serrano, red bell pepper, garlic, white wine vinegar, avocado oil, salt).
dishes.guy_response: It's really delicious. It's very juicy. You could take that meat and do about anything you want with it. You can make a sandwich out of it. It's very good. The koubideh is wonderful. With ground chuck in particular, if you're actually grounding it yourself, that's why it tastes so much better. It tastes fresh. The chimichurri sauce, tangy, very savory. It teleports me to a new place.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Mediterranean Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

dishes.description: Phyllo dough layered with a filling of sautéed white onion, green onions, dill, eggs, spinach, feta, salt, pepper, baked until golden and flaky.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, that's great. The dill, the dill actually really makes that. You know what it does? It complements really nice with the feta. Mm, that's good, dude. It's simple, done right. I'll eat that again. I'll eat that 36 more times. It's nice and creamy like quiche. And I love the layers of the crust. Very tasty.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Buffalo, New York with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here on the south side of Buffalo, New York, in a predominantly Irish neighborhood where we've got a great story about two brothers. Kevin, or Louse as he was known, was part owner of this neighborhood pub. Unfortunately, in 2002, he passed away. Now, no one wanted to see the pub leave the family, so his brother Pat, a local fireman, along with his brother-in-law, took over. One of the things we love on Triple D is tradition. Time to check out Blackthorn Restaurant and Pub.

dishes.description: Roast beef cooked medium-rare with mirepoix, browning agent, Montreal steak seasoning, and rosemary, served on a kimmelweck roll with caraway seeds and kosher salt, dipped in au jus.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Wow. That is so tender. Big flavor. Crazy with the salt and the caraway. That au jus is awesome too. Mmm. I could only probably eat about a thousand of those. Nice job, man. Beef on whack is so delicious. Meat's perfect. It's tender. There's no grizzle, no nothing. No schnizzle on the grizzle.

dishes.dish_name: Irish Beer Cheddar and Potato Soup
dishes.description: A more than 30-year-old recipe, this soup is made with butter, Spanish onion, Irish beer, parboiled potatoes, white pepper, Montreal seasoning, seasoned salt, chicken base, shredded cheddar and Monterey Jack cheese, heavy cream, and a touch of nacho cheese sauce for color and thickening.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, that's big, deep, rich flavor. Creamy. Just has like a perfume of the beer. That is the most beer I've ever seen in a beer cheddar soup. Really good. Good job, man.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Buffalo, New York with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Let me throw some numbers at you. How about 7, 11, and 200? See, I'm in Lackawanna, New York, right by Buffalo, where this guy grew up and lived in seven different houses in this neighborhood. Now, over the course of 11 years, he made over 200 trips to New York City to sample, taste, and research the finest Italian restaurants. Now, you add up those numbers, what do you get? 218, right? No, that's not what I'm talking about. What you get is a neighborhood guy that comes back to his home turf and is serving up. Hand-made Italian food. Welcome to Mulberry Italian Ristorante.

dishes.description: Meatballs made with 80-20 ground beef and fresh ground pork, fresh ground breadcrumbs, Parmesan cheese, fresh basil, curly parsley, garlic, dry oregano, salt, black pepper, extra virgin olive oil, eggs, milk, and warm water, then baked.
dishes.guy_response: That's a great meatball, man. Really nice and tender. Melts in your mouth. Big flavor. Cut it with a fork. It stays together, yet when you put it in your mouth, it dissolves. Delicious.

dishes.description: Handmade lasagna with double zero flour pasta, layered with tomato sauce, sliced meatballs, mozzarella and Parmesan cheese, chicken, stuffed hot pepper mixture, and ricotta cheese (tossed with parsley and garlic), baked at 375 degrees.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Wow, man. Good? That's not good. That's great. It's rich. It's flavorful. Everything you want a lasagna to be. And quality. Mmm. My man. Nice job, Joe. I have to get you your tattoo. That's some gangster lasagna right there. I don't whip that out all the time. That lasagna, that deserves it. Just a lot of meat. It's nice and spicy. It's got the hot pepper.

visits.guy_intro: So what do you call a joint that is an English pub with a little Filipino flair? You're right. This is George and the Dragon.

dishes.description: Onion jam (caramelized onions with brown sugar, balsamic vinegar, and water) mixed with whipped cream cheese, mayonnaise, Worcestershire sauce, salt, cayenne pepper, Coleman's dry mustard, and Parmesan cheese. Topped with cooked cold bacon and more Parmesan, then baked at 350 degrees for five minutes. Finished with chives.
dishes.guy_response: That's ridiculous. You know what? This should be illegal. It's not just onions and bacon. There's much more depth. I think you know what really makes it? Is that mustard. A little tang of that. Very good. Excellent. Nice job.

visits.video_title: Top 5 #DDD Videos in Minnesota with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Did you know they have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place where they're doing off-the-hook pizza? This is Pizzeria Lola. A lot of unusual flavors. Sliced and served up by owner Ann Kim, who grew up learning to cook from her Korean grandma before discovering another passion a little later in life.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Bacon, Sausage & Ham Videos with Guy Fieri
visits.guy_intro: Did you know they have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place, where they're doing off-the-hook pizza? This is Pizzeria Lola.

visits.guy_intro: They have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired, French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place, where they're doing off-the-hook pizza. This is Pizzeria Lola.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Minneapolis with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Here's your mascheroni. It's just kind of like a meat overload.

visits.video_title: Top 30 #DDD Videos of ALL TIME with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Did you know they have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place where they're doing off-the-hook pizza? This is Pizzeria Lola.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Korean-Inspired Food Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Did you know they have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place where they're doing off-the-hook pizza? This is Pizzeria Lola. They have a bunch of pizzas. They have pizzas that are really out there and innovative. Including a tribute to Anne's deep Korean roots. The Lady Zaza.

visits.video_title: Wildest Meat Lover Pizza Videos on #DDD with Guy Fieri 🥩🍕 Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Did you know they have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired, French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place where they're doing off-the-hook pizza? This is Pizzeria Lola.

dishes.description: Pizza made with a 48-hour fermented dough (poolish method). Topped with house red sauce (California tomatoes, red pear tomatoes, puree, salt, dried basil, dried oregano, granulated garlic, onion powder), Wisconsin mozzarella, house-made fennel sausage (ground Berkshire pork, toasted fennel seeds, fresh chopped garlic, chili flake, salt, pepper), and natural casing pepperoni. Cooked in a copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza oven (Le Pagnole) at 550-600 degrees.
dishes.guy_response: We got this heirloom pork with the real fat in the real flavor and the fennels rocking this. A lot of people say, oh, sausage and pepperoni. I've had that all the time. This is way beyond your everyday pizza joint on the corner. Sausage and pepperoni. You got to try this one. It's the most heightened, delicious pepperoni sausage pizza you've ever had. You're like, this is what pepperoni is supposed to look like.

dishes.description: A pizza with a two-day fermented dough, house red sauce, Wisconsin mozzarella, house-made fennel sausage, and natural casing pepperoni, cooked in a wood-fired oven.
dishes.guy_response: We got this heirloom pork with the real fat in it and the real flavor. And the fennel is rocking this. ... This is way beyond your everyday pizza joint on the corner, sausage and pepperoni. You got to try this one. It's the most heightened, delicious pepperoni sausage pizza you've ever had. You're like, this is what pepperoni is supposed to taste like.

dishes.description: Pizza with red California tomatoes as base, house-made Korean sausage (Berkshire pork, salt, pepper, sugar, ginger, garlic, Korean chili), kimchi (napa cabbage brined, then mixed with Korean chili powder, fish sauce, scallion, brined shrimp, garlic, ginger, sugar, rice paste, gochugaru, cilantro, scallions), serrano peppers, fresh scallion, finished with sesame oil, soy chili glaze, and toasted black and white sesame seeds.
dishes.guy_response: Wow. A little bit of sweet, a little salt. You got some crunch. There's onions, a little bit of the serrano. We got dragon sauce on top. Out of all joints we've tried, without question, this is way up there. Just dynamite.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Tries Lao Fried Rice Ball in California 🇱🇦 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: We're in California, diving into a different version of fried rice, the nam kao, which is a lot crunchier and a really interesting dish.

dishes.description: Fried rice balls made with jasmine rice, red curry paste, sugar, onions, grated coconut, chicken base soup, salt, oyster sauce, and egg, deep-fried and then broken up to form a salad with pork skin, cured fermented pork (Lao Nam), sugar, lime juice, cilantro, peanuts, and scallions, served as a lettuce wrap with ginger and mint.
dishes.guy_response: This is texture city. You get the sweet, the salty, or the little spice from the chili. It's very delicious.

dishes.description: Shredded green papaya muddled with garlic, Thai chili, green beans, palm sugar, fish sauce, fresh lime juice, tomato, and crushed roasted peanuts.
dishes.guy_response: The sugar, the fish sauce, the lime juice, the Thai chili, the garlic. All muddled together with that fantastic texture of the green papaya that just soaks it up. It's like the best cold noodle salad you've ever had. This will change your life.

dishes.description: A seven-inch baguette hollowed out, spread with garlic mayo and house-made chicken liver pate, filled with head cheese (pig face, pork neck, ham cooked down), barbecue pork, Vietnamese bologna (jalua), pickled carrots and daikon, cucumbers, jalapenos, and cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Great bread. I mean, there's just so many different components. The balance is great. As you chew it, you just get, oh, there it is. Oh, now I got some sour. Oh, now I got some heat. Now I got a little jalapeno. Now I got the freshness of the cucumber. It just keeps trumping itself as you enjoy it. I dig it.

dishes.description: Meatballs made from trimmed pork loin, bay shrimp, and crab, seasoned with sugar, oyster sauce, Golden Mountain seasoning, garlic, pepper, and eggs, wrapped in wonton wrappers, then steamed and optionally deep-fried. Served with a sauce made from soy sauce, vinegar, Worcestershire, sugar, and chili, topped with jalapeno, cilantro, and toasted garlic.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That sauce, nice vinegar balance. Wicked good. Those are killer. Dynamite. Not too spicy, not too salty. These are really good. Excites the palate. It really does.

dishes.description: A crepe made from water, rice flour, turmeric, curry powder, and coconut cream, cooked on a hot pan, filled with cabbage, carrots, chicken, and white tiger shrimp. Served with a traditional sauce of sugar, water, fish sauce, vinegar, pureed garlic, and sambal, and eaten as a lettuce wrap with cilantro and mint.
dishes.guy_response: This is my jam right here. It's so delicious with the crunchy vegetables, the crispiness of the crepe. The pancake is so light but crunchy. With that sauce, it's not super sweet. It's tangy, a little bit of spice to it.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Maryland with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Baltimore, Maryland, about two miles from downtown in the Hamden neighborhood. Now, we're right in the center of all the universities. Now, I hear that they've got a place where you sit at the counter as the chefs cook. The dude that owns it is from Italy, and the place is legit. This is Grano Pasta Bar. At only 400 square feet, this joint's been making room for just one thing since Naples-born chef Gino Troia opened in 08.

dishes.description: Clam dish with a lovely light sauce, a touch of wine, spinach, and tomatoes, served with pasta.
dishes.guy_response: You better deliver, and you sure delivered, man. That is awesome. Fantastic. Just has a great amount of herbs in the sauce.

dishes.description: Handmade ravioli with a cream sauce, wild mushrooms, and sprinkles of truffles on top.
dishes.guy_response: It's rich. It's light. It's earthy. That's outrageous. I mean, you hand make everything. The pasta, the filling. Delicious dish. Thank you. Outrageous. It just almost melts in your mouth. When you taste it, the mushrooms and the natural flavors come through.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Maryland with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So, here I am in the Roland Park neighborhood of Baltimore, Maryland on Cold Spring Lane. Well, more fondly known as Eddie Dobkin Way. That's because back in the 90s, Eddie had like five rockin' restaurants in this area. And the one he's most widely known for is a funky big breakfast joint known as Miss Shirley's Cafe.

dishes.description: Two large pieces of challah bread stuffed with an amazing coconut cream.
dishes.guy_response: Each bite you get the texture of the coconut, the sweetness with the crunch. I have no problem giving it a wow-wee. And, uh... Did she just drop a wow-wee on me? Wow-wee. It's amazing. Apparently that's something you might have heard when Eddie was around. One of his favorite expressions was wow-wee. So this guy was a fan of hers. And... This guy was alive at the party. This is a wow-wee.

dishes.description: Jumbo lump crab meat crab cake, made with Old Bay, mayo, eggs, mustard, salt, roasted red peppers, parsley, and cracker meal, served on a sandwich with Tabasco bacon onion jam, lettuce, tomatoes, and Old Bay remoulade.
dishes.guy_response: This is outrageous.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Maryland with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: E Pasquale in Baltimore is a takeout deli. Turned sit down joint. For home cooked favorites. I'll come here, get some nice pasta, salads, and things like that. Definitely some of the best food I've ever had. I've never had a lasagna like this. Out of this world. I feel like I'm at. from Italy, right?

dishes.description: Arborio rice balls stuffed with a beef and pork mixture, marinara, and mozzarella, then floured, breaded, and fried until golden brown. Served with marinara sauce.
dishes.guy_response: The rice has got a nice little firm center to it with a nice creaminess on the outside. That is just fantastic. I love the peas in there too. That's about it right there. Winner, winner, rice ball dinner. First thing that hits me is the crispiness of the outer crust. The meat and the rice and the mozzarella all blend together. It's really good.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Maryland with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So this leg of the trip, you rode in and sent me to Baltimore, to the kind of places we love doing on Triple D. A big, shiny, family owned diner serving homemade everything from everywhere. Hey, welcome to the Broadway Diner.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: You wrote in and sent me to Baltimore. To the kind of places we love doing on Triple D. A big, shiny, family-owned diner serving homemade everything from everywhere. Hey, welcome to the Broadway Diner.

dishes.description: A seafood dish featuring scallops, lobster tails, shrimp, and mussels, cooked with white wine, garlic, fresh tomato, basil, and marinara sauce, served over al dente pasta with cheese.
dishes.guy_response: The pasta's al dente. I go to Italian restaurants across the country, I can't find pasta that's al dente. I have nothing to say, man, other than just wow. Delicious. That's pretty much it in a word.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Maryland with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: When you're in Maryland and you're thinking traditional food, well, you're probably thinking crab, especially the way they steam them and dump a pile of them on butcher paper and everybody dives in. So I'm 20 minutes south of Baltimore rolling in for what they call a crab feast at a place called the Stony Creek Inn.

dishes.description: Blue crabs steamed by the basket with a custom seasoning blend (salt, paprika, dry mustard, chili flake, cayenne, thyme) and served with sweet corn.
dishes.guy_response: They're very rich. It's buttery. It's sweet. The salt compliments it really well. The seasoning on the crab is pretty outstanding. Wow.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Maryland with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: In 1927, a Greek immigrant by the name of Gregory Theokoumakos opened a hot dog stand in Baltimore serving Coney Island-style dogs with his own chili sauce recipe. Now, it was a hit. I mean, a big hit. And 81 years later, it still is. And it all began with owner Andy Ferrantos' great uncle Gregory, who partnered up with his cousin Alex and started a tradition.

dishes.description: Hot dog loaded with homemade Coney sauce (chili), onions, and ketchup, served on a steamed bun.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That's great. Nice and juicy dog. Crazy meat concoction on top. That's good. That's good. That's good. That's good. That's good. That's good. White onion's perfect. Bun's nice and steamed. It'll cost $1.90. That's it. If I was a college student, I could live here. You taste the hot dog. You taste the onions. You taste the chili. Delicious. It's the best hot dog I've ever had.

dishes.description: French fries served Baltimore style with homemade gravy.
dishes.guy_response: Can I try one? There's nothing wrong with that at all. That's right. I'd dip the dog in that.

visits.video_title: Guy Dives Into a LEGENDARY Cheeseburger in NYC 🍔 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

dishes.description: Classic 70-30 beef patty, cooked on a super hot cast iron skillet, topped with American cheese and caramelized onions, served on a pedestrian bun.
dishes.guy_response: Crust of two billows from that screaming, smoking hot cast iron skillet. I like the juiciness of it. The cheese melting into it. The first time I bit in that burger, I was amazed. It tastes unlike any other burger in New York. I think it's really juicy and really excellent.

dishes.description: 8-ounce grass-fed brisket and chuck blend patty, topped with roasted bone marrow, raclette cheese, and a compound butter with rosemary, parsley, and roasted garlic, served on a brioche bun.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, man. Never tasted anything like this. The richness of the meat inside of it. Oh, yeah. Dynamite brioche bun, by the way. It has this very composed. Elegant dish profile from the herbaceousness of the butter. And there are clutcheese. Silky, creamy, a little tangy. It's beyond a burger's level. It's dynamite, man. Mid-rare bone marrow burger. It's the marrow all the way. It just melts in your mouth. It's wonderful. So juicy. The herbal butter kind of just like oozes through the meat. The bun is awesome. It's gluten-free. Wow. This is the place.

name: Gastro Grind
owner_chef: Greg and Belinda Rangel, Ruben Reyes

visits.video_title: Guy Dives Into a LEGENDARY Cheeseburger in NYC 🍔 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: We're here to check out a joint where a husband and wife and a stepson are doing burgers outside of the box. This is Gastro Grind.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Burger Videos in the Western US | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: We're here to check out a joint where a husband and wife and a stepson are doing burgers outside of the box. This is Gastro Grind.

dishes.description: High-quality ground patty topped with lobster, lemon aioli (with preserved lemons, garlic puree, Dijon), romesco sauce (with piquillo peppers, garlic puree, croutons, almonds, parsley), and garlic anchovy aioli, served on a potato bun with free-sale lettuce and crispy onions.
dishes.guy_response: There's so much big flavor in this. With the romesco, the brie, the anchovy aioli, then on top of it with the lobster, a very sophisticated burger. Your flavors all coincide together. They work together very well. Good texture to it, too. That's the real deal right there. That's the one.

dishes.description: Three patties made with ground croutons, raw diced onions, raw garlic, cumin, chili powder, and eggs, topped with provolone cheese, caramelized onions, sauteed mushrooms, house-made marinara sauce, and house-made pickles, served with hand-cut fries.
dishes.guy_response: Great meatball flavor. You can barely get your mouth around it, but it's really good. Holy moly. Look at that. Love the sauteed mushrooms. Nice little kiss of the marinara. That right there screaming Italian family Sunday dinner, that's ridiculously big. The sandwiches are like complete meals. It's got some southern kick to it, and it makes it totally awesome.

visits.video_title: Guy Dives Into a LEGENDARY Cheeseburger in NYC 🍔 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Everything's made in-house here. And they infuse their beers in a lot of their menu items. Even down to their not-so-basic burgers.

dishes.description: Sliders with herb goat cheese, house-made brioche buns, bacon jam (with bacon, shallots, brown sugar, red pepper flakes, honey, house-made beer), tri-blend patty (chuck, porter, brisket), roasted tomatoes, fresh arugula, and beer barbecue sauce (with garlic, onion, tomato paste, Worcestershire, brown sugar, yellow mustard, apple cider vinegar, plum tomato, blonde ale).
dishes.guy_response: The burger, burger, burger is really yummy. Probably one of the best burger sliders I've ever had. I love the goat cheese and the bacon jam. That's legit going down. It's not good unless you have to lick your fingers. What you put into one little burger is more than a lot of people put into their own menu. I mean, you've got such great flavors. The goat cheese is cool. It's tangy, good acid. You got a lot of sweet coming up from the bacon jam. Love the fat in the burger. The bun's delicious. It's nice. It's light. Flavor, design, attention to detail, creativity. Well done. Great burger. Burger, burger, burger up. Definitely. This is not your average Slider.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm south of Houston, almost by Galveston, in a little town called Kima. But there's also this great little eatery here, T-Bone Tom's, where I hear these folks have been hand-cutting their steaks for over 35 years.

dishes.description: Marinated ribeye steak, hand-cut and charbroiled to medium-rare perfection.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm, that's a great steak. That really is. Wow, that's a nice piece of meat.

dishes.description: House-made Polish sausage, 50% beef and 50% pork, stuffed and smoked on the pit.
dishes.guy_response: Hear the little snap of that casing? And look at the juice coming out of that. Mmm. That is about as tender a sausage I've ever tried.

dishes.description: Deep-fried pickled jalapenos stuffed with smoked brisket and homemade barbecue sauce, double-dredged in flour, buttermilk, and panko.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Oh, yeah. Wow. I mean, the barbecue's great. The jalapeno being a pickled jalapeno really makes it. That's killer. I mean, killer, killer.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: All you got to do is slow down and take a look around, like here in Rosenberg, Texas, 30 miles southwest of Houston, where an abandoned gas station has morphed into Bob's Taco Station and Snowballs.

dishes.description: Cubed blade meat cooked with red peppers, onion, green bell pepper, and tomato, seasoned with a spice mix, served on a homemade flour tortilla.
dishes.guy_response: The meat, super tender. Got some creaminess to it. I love seeing the peppers inside of it.

dishes.description: Pork butt simmered until tender, shredded, mixed with lard, pureed garlic, cumin, pepper, and guajillo pepper, then wrapped in masa and corn husks, steamed.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Delicious. That pork. Nice, moist, right amount of seasoning. I like the meat ratio.

dishes.description: Finely shaved ice flavored with Dreamsicle syrup and cream, served like a snow cone.
dishes.guy_response: Dude, this is nuts. It tastes just like a 50-50 bar. This is nothing like a snow cone.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: We're going to start off with a little joint out of Berkeley called 900 Grayson.

dishes.dish_name: Ladyboy Thai Noodle Salad with Grilled Shrimp
dishes.description: Rice noodles with mango, zucchini, carrot, cucumber, daikon, mint, and basil, tossed in a dressing, topped with grilled marinated shrimp, sriracha aioli, rice powder, chili oil, and basil oil.
dishes.guy_response: The basil oil and the chili. Chili oil are right there. But you know what really is the kicker for me on that? That toasted rice. Oh, that's good.

dishes.description: Fried chicken breast served on a buttermilk waffle, topped with a creamy vegetarian gravy and Aleppo pepper.
dishes.guy_response: Savory. You get the salt and the crunch of the chicken. Then you get into the waffle. OK, this is going to be sweet. But then you. You nail it with that gravy, dude. That is a wham bam combo.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I think it's time that I took some great advice from my friend Maria and try some Peruvian food. And that brings me here to Taste of Peru.

dishes.description: Masa dough made with corn and rice flour, flavored with achiote oil, onion, garlic paste, chili paste, chicken bouillon, pepper, salt, and chicken stock. Filled with chicken, hot pepper, peanuts, and olive, then wrapped in banana leaf and foil, and boiled.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, that's awesome. It's the best masa I've ever tried. I think that's because of the rice flour.

dishes.description: Ribeye strips seasoned with pepper, salt, garlic, and soy sauce, stir-fried in a special Peruvian pan with onion, tomatoes, vinegar, and beer. Served with french fries and rice.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, humbucker. Fantastic. Oh, my gosh. The way the pan was so hot, you broke the tomato down so fast. So it works into the sauce, but it's not a tomato sauce.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: We're here in Charlotte, North Carolina to check out a drive-in. This is South 21, serving Charlotte since 1955.

dishes.description: Classic burger patty served on a toasted bun with mustard, slaw, scratch-made chili, and a pickle.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. That's great. That has all of the key pieces that a great burger needs to have. The cool slaw, the bite of the onion, and then you get the chili with a little bit of spice. That is burger 101 right there, baby.

dishes.description: Freshly cut onion rings, with centers removed, double-breaded in a milk/egg/flour batter and non-salted cracker meal, then deep-fried until golden brown.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Really nice onion. The batter's nice and crunchy. I want some salt on it. Little ketchup. Just like I said about the Superboy, that right there is onion ring 101.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in Berkeley, California, to check out Betty's Ocean View Diner.

dishes.description: Pork hocks and pork butt cooked to make a stock, meat shredded and ground, then mixed with stock, cayenne, sage, black pepper, white and yellow cornmeal. Chilled, sliced, and pan-fried in clarified butter. Served with grilled tomatoes and homemade applesauce.
dishes.guy_response: If that's what scrapple tasted like everywhere across the country, I'd eat scrapple every breakfast. The crunch on this is crunch-tastic.

dishes.description: Fresh rock cod dredged in seasoned flour (salt, pepper, Old Bay), pan-fried in clarified butter. Served on a toasted baguette with lettuce and homemade tartar sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Now that's how you're supposed to do it. Taste the fish. A really nice, complimentary tartar sauce that doesn't steal all the thunder.

dishes.description: Batter made with egg yolks, sugar, salt, clarified butter, half and half, and flour. Egg whites are folded in per order, then pan-fried in clarified butter and finished in the oven.
dishes.guy_response: Now see, this is nice and light and airy. This is like a pancake meets a light English muffin. Good flavor, nice texture on the top.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: These folks are serving up some Southern classics. This is Dish.

dishes.description: Whole chicken roasted and shredded. Stock made from bones, skin, white onion, pepper, celery salt, granulated garlic, and onion. Dumplings made from flour, eggs, and melted butter, cooked in the stock and thickened with a roux.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Awesome. Really awesome. We got a super tender dumpling. And I like the fact that you roasted the chicken, so it's got real chicken flavor. That is down home chicken and dumplings.

dishes.description: Fresh salmon diced and mixed with parsley, pulverized onions, whisked eggs, mustard, mayonnaise, celery, lemon juice, lemon zest, dill weed, and white breadcrumbs. Formed into patties, rolled in panko, and pan-fried. Served with honey orange butter.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That's nice. Dill right off the bat. Salmon comes in. There's a lot of crunch on that cake. It's just really nice. The nice little touch of adding the butter brings it all together.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Today we're on the water in Santa Cruz, California, to check out a third-generation seafood restaurant that started as a bait and tackle shop. It's time to dock up to Aldo's.

dishes.description: Homemade pasta dough filled with a mixture of boiled spinach and Swiss chard, focaccia breadcrumbs, sautéed onions, Romano and Parmesan cheeses, eggs, and olive oil. Served with marinara sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Awesome. If there's a time that I really want fresh pasta, it's when it comes in a ravioli. The two cheeses, the Swiss chard. That's impressive.

dishes.description: Seafood stew with rock cod, shrimp, mussels, clams, calamari, and crab legs, cooked in a marinara sauce with butter, chili pepper, garlic, capers, and white wine.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Everything is nice and tender. You can taste the seafood inside of the sauce. Great flavor. It's all around. It's really spicy.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Well, here's the oasis in Charlotte, North Carolina. Landmark diner.

dishes.description: Turkey boiled with onions, celery, carrots, pepper, salt, and water. Served with gravy made from turkey stock, butter, flour, salt, and black pepper. Stuffing made with vegetable oil, onions, celery, carrots, butter, apple mixture, garlic, sage, black pepper, salt, sugar, poultry seasoning, challah bread, and eggs.
dishes.guy_response: Hm. Wow, that's big flavor, man. Tender turkey, big flavor in the gravy. I tell you, man, it's good. That's Thanksgiving on a plate every day.

dishes.description: Sautéed white onions, green onions, and dill mixed with eggs, spinach, and feta cheese. Layered between filo dough sheets brushed with olive oil and butter, then baked.
dishes.guy_response: That's great. The dill. The dill actually really makes that. It complements really nice with the feta. That's good, dude. It's simple, done right.

visits.video_title: BINGE the Best of #DDD Season 8 with Guy Fieri 🔥 | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Starting off with old school Hawaiian cooking at a place called Highway Inn.

dishes.description: Pork belly and salted butterfish wrapped in taro leaves and tea leaves, tied, then steamed for two hours. Served with poi.
dishes.guy_response: It is much, much better. Mm. That's good. The fat really mellows it out. And you got a little bit amount of salt in there. I love it.

dishes.description: Chuck roll sliced, marinated with salt and shoyu (soy sauce), then hung above stoves to dry for four hours. Pan-fried on a flat top and chopped.

dishes.description: Housemade tempeh, fermented soybeans, cut and dipped in a batter with garlic powder, salt, pepper, egg, water, and scallions, then deep-fried. Served with achar, a side of spicy pickled vegetables including cabbage, cucumber, red peppers, carrots, and pineapple.

dishes.description: Country chicken boiled for 2 hours with ginger paste (candlenut, shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal, turmeric, turmeric powder), bruised lemongrass, Indonesian bay leaf, turmeric, salt. Coconut milk added and cooked for 20 mins. Chicken is strained, dried, and then fried with the skimmed ginger paste. Served with rice and sambal terasi (Thai chilies, shrimp paste, shallots, tomatoes, lime leaf, red peppers, sugar, salt, paprika, blended and cooked).
dishes.guy_response: I love the yard bird. This little ginger fry crunch that's on the outside, I keep chasing it around because I want more of it. The sambal is the bomb. With the rice, with the crunch, with the fry, with the chicken, when you can get that optimum bite, it was dynamite.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Feasts On From-Scratch Bagels in NOLA 🥯 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Here I am in New Orleans, so you're expecting Bourbon Street jambalaya and beignets. But no, no, no. Today you get a neighborhood and a juice bar that makes homemade bagels and lox. Because here at Suri's, it's all about owner Greg Suri's favorites.

dishes.description: Homemade bagels, boiled and baked like in New York, served with fresh, salty lox and cream cheese.
dishes.guy_response: It's wonderful. Plus the lox are nice and salty. It's a whole different animal when they're fresh like that. Oh, Lord. Mmm. Tastes fresh. It's much more doughy. You can tell that it's homemade. Lox are great. It's ridiculously good. The texture's perfect. Goes well with the cream cheese. Really, really good. I could sit there and eat that whole thing.

dishes.dish_name: Corned Beef Hash with Andouille and Boudin Sausage
dishes.description: Homemade corned beef, smoked boudin sausage, andouille sausage, rosemary, pre-roasted potatoes, and Cajun seasoning.
dishes.guy_response: That is great. Wow, that's good. That's hash-alicious. Top three best hashes. It's nice and chunky. That's what I like about it.

dishes.dish_name: Apple Ginger Juice with Wheatgrass Shooter
dishes.description: Fresh squeezed apple ginger juice with an optional wheatgrass shooter.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, you pick up the ginger in that. It's not a sipping kind of beverage. It's good. I mean, it's tasty. It's a little shocking at first. It's amazing.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Feasts On From-Scratch Bagels in NOLA 🥯 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Gainesville, Florida. I have been in this parking lot, 4th Avenue Food Park. No, I was looking for some wood-fired pizza, but then I heard the wood-fired pizza team also does bagels, and not just any bagels. The claim to fame is they're Florida-style bagels. Oh, I love me some bagels. This is Humble Wood-Fired Bagels.

dishes.description: Perfectly cured and smoked salmon lox on a toasted salted rosemary bagel with lemon dill cream cheese, capers, cucumbers, onions, and sprouts.
dishes.guy_response: Bagel's dynamite. Great chew. Really like the rosemary. A legit bagel. The salmon, the color's beautiful. A little bit of smoke to it. If you're a smoked salmon, lox and bagels kind of person, this is your game. And the sprouts on top are perfect. The smoked lox blends well with the dill, cream cheese that they use.

dishes.description: Roasted and marinated carrot lox on a toasted salted rosemary bagel with cashew cream cheese, capers, cucumber, pickled onions, and sprouts.
dishes.guy_response: First time I've had vegan lox. The carrot, that is a flavor bomb. That cashew spread sings right through. They're both dynamite. I would love to know their secrets for how they make those carrots. Because they've got that sort of smoky finish that you expect from a lox.

dishes.description: Crimini mushrooms, shiitake bacon, three olive cream cheese, mushroom tapenade, arugula, and Swiss cheese on an everything bagel, melted.
dishes.guy_response: If anybody gets bored by a bagel, come down here to Humble. You get this big umami bomb. You get some of the acid, some of the brininess, the three olive smear, really salty and briny cream cheese. I love the arugula on it. And on top of it all is the everything bagel underneath. Wow, that's good. The team here just does magic with mushrooms. The everything bagel is like a little bit crispy. It's perfect like that. This is a destination bagel joint.

This combo is brought to you by K-Heritage, who's doubling up oven duty. Bagels in the morning. And then it's pizzas in the afternoon.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Korean-Inspired Food Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So we're here in Savannah, Georgia, with the one and only Iron Chef Stephanie Izar. Chef, are you hungry? Always. Fantastic. We're here. I had a food truck that's not even open.
