This is Big Bon Bodega.

dishes.description: Toasted sesame bagel with kimchi cream cheese, bulgogi meat, American cheese, pickled onions, and kimchi.
dishes.guy_response: The bagel is dynamite. It's got great chew. It's amazing. The bulgogi, you can put that on a flip-flop. But it's a really well-constructed sandwich. There's the right amount, considering the amount of bagel that there is. I mean, it's dynamite. The beef was so tender. And it just had a little bit of a spice. The combination of the meat and the kimchi, really excellent.

dishes.description: Sesame bagel with kimchi cream cheese, bulgogi meat, American cheese, pickled onions, and kimchi. Bulgogi meat is flank steak marinated in garlic, ginger, sesame oil, soy sauce, cola, brown sugar. Kimchi is napa cabbage brined, then mixed with a paste of red bell pepper, salted shrimp, fish sauce, garlic, ginger, sugar, rice paste, gochugaru, cilantro, scallions. Kimchi cream cheese is cream cheese, chopped kimchi, sambal, green onions, gochugaru, fresh ginger.
dishes.guy_response: The bagel is dynamite. It's got great chew. I wouldn't have thought to put American cheese on this with the cream cheese, but it makes that Philly cheesesteak sort of flavor. And then those red onions just cut through all of that richness. It's amazing. The bulgogi, you can put that on a flip-flop, but it's a really well-constructed sandwich. I mean, I almost thought you were going to have too much of the kimchi cream cheese, but there's the right amount of considering the amount of bagel that there is. I mean, it's dynamite.

dishes.description: Homemade jalapeno cheddar bagel, boiled and baked, with a serious kick, served with vegetable cream cheese.

That crust, that chew, soft in the middle, nice texture on the outside, real deal, brother. Real deal. Perfect crunch on the top. The cheddar has, like, a nice mildness to it that counteracts with the jalapeno.

Bacon's great. But these oven roasted plum tomatoes, the acid of that cut with the fat and the spiciness of the jalapenos and poblanos in the cream cheese, you're a dangerous woman. dishes.description: Brisket brined for 15 days with kosher salt, brown sugar, white sugar, pink salt, honey, pickling spice, and garlic. Not too much smoke. Not too much pepper. Coriander. Not too salty. I mean, just a package deal, man. That is an incredibly righteous pastrami sandwich.

dishes.description: Boneless chicken thighs marinated overnight in egg, buttermilk, flour, pepper, salt, hot sauce. Double-dredged in seasoned flour (salt, black pepper, white pepper, cayenne, smoked paprika, onion powder, garlic powder, baking powder). Double-fried. Served on a toasted bun with lime aioli, kimchi, ssamjang, house pickles.
dishes.guy_response: My friend, that is an excellent fried chicken. Excellent fried chicken. That is super crunchy-tacious. I have a chicken joint called Chicken Guy, and I say that sauce is the boss. When I watch you put on all that ssamjang, I'm like, just call it the ssamjang sandwich. But it's this balance that goes on between the fat of that lime aioli with the acid of the pickle. It's like clashing right there in the middle of the chicken sandwich. Ba-boom.

dishes.description: Combination of fried shrimp and roast beef. Roast beef is top round, boiled with kosher salt, black pepper, whole peppercorns, garlic. Demi-glace made from roasted veal bones, tomato paste, rosemary, thyme, red wine, bay leaf, reduced for 4 hours. Remoulade sauce (mayo, creole mustard, garlic puree, cajun seasoning, hot sauce, lemon juice, olive oil). Shrimp dredged in buttermilk and flour, fried. Served on Leidenheimer bread with remoulade, lettuce, tomatoes, house pickles, garlic aioli, and beef gravy.
dishes.guy_response: One minute you're getting crunchy shrimp and remoulade. And then you transition right into this delicious warm shredded beef with the gravy. Rich in flavor. Leidenheimer makes all the difference in the world. Pickled veg is a must. A little unconventional. This is your house. Like my good buddy Steve Hutchinson said, I learned some stuff I knew and I learned some stuff I didn't knew. Delicious, man. Not a thing I would change.

dishes.description: Carnitas (Boston butt, fat removed, cut small, fried in pork lard with Mexican bellies, allspice, orange, simmered for a couple hours, then shredded). Chilorio sauce (ancho and pasilla peppers toasted, steeped in hot water, deseeded, blended with Mexican oregano, comino, vinegar, garlic cloves, salt, strained, fried in pork lard). Carnitas cooked in sauce. Served on homemade bread with refried beans, queso, jalapenos, tomatoes, white onions, marinated onions, cabbage, cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: It's out of bounds, dude. The freshness of the bread right off the bat. Then you get this hot, little bit spicy, the meat. Then you get the crunch of the onion. A little snap from the jalapeno. This has got to be one of the best tortas I've ever had. All right. I mean, one of the best, dude.

dishes.description: Triple-decker sandwich with Texas toast. Chicken (rubbed with salt, granulated garlic, sugar, white pepper, black pepper, whole thyme, onion powder, dill weed, paprika, chili powder, cumin, celery salt, poultry seasoning, cornstarch, water, cooked in oven). Swiss cheese, cheddar cheese, ham. Dipped in egg, breaded with panko breadcrumbs, deep-fried. Served with raspberry coulis (raspberry preserves, cranberry juice).
dishes.guy_response: Money up. The crispiness comes out in the way that they batter it. When you bite into it, it just explodes in your mouth. It's really juicy. And that flavor comes through. Then you dip it in some jam. It's incredible.

dishes.description: House-made cavatelli pasta with fresh Florida lobster, celery, carrots, onions, garlic, black pepper, brandy, reduced lobster stock, heavy cream, roux, asiago, fontina, parmesan, mozzarella, smoked provolone, and truffle breadcrumbs.
dishes.guy_response: That lobster is out of bounds.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: When you're in Maryland and you're thinking traditional food, well, you're probably thinking crab. Especially the way they steam them and dump a pile of them on butcher paper. And everybody dives in. So I'm 20 minutes south of Baltimore, rolling in for what they call a crab feast at a place called the Stony Creek Inn.

dishes.description: Blue crabs steamed with a house seasoning blend (no Old Bay), served with sweet corn.
dishes.guy_response: It's just fantastic. They're very rich. It's buttery. It's sweet. The salt complements it really well. The seasoning on the crab is pretty outstanding. Wow.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I am hanging out at La Tostararia with Chef Fernando. This guy's got a story that you will love. Comes from Mexico, goes to Canada, opens a taqueria, comes down to the United States, opens a carnitas joint here in Grand Central Market, then goes to culinary school, elevates himself in the world of food, and opens up La Tostararia.

dishes.description: A seafood burger made with wild Mexican shrimp and Oaxaca cheese, served on a bun with chipotle aioli, lettuce, tomato, onion, and an avocado-mango-radish salsa, topped with shredded potatoes for crunch.
dishes.guy_response: Good news is that's fantastic. The bad news is the crew's hitting the vending machine. When you bite into it, you get the saltiness of the cheese, the texture of the cheese. The shrimp's cooked perfectly. And then all the accoutrement, the shredded lettuce, the chipotle mayo, the tomato, the raw onion, the phenomenal salsa with the mango and the avocado. I pride myself in my burgers. I'm known for my burgers. That right there, in my expectation level of burgers, that is about as high as they go. Thank you. Outstanding.

name: The Grilled Cheese and Crab Cake Company

visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in South New Jersey in a town called Summers Point. Now, this is where people cruise through to go down to the boardwalk in Ocean City. But more importantly, to visit this joint. This is the Grilled Cheese and Crab Cake Company.

dishes.description: A grilled cheese sandwich with a crab cake in the middle. The crab cake is made with jumbo lump crab meat, mayo, eggs, lemon juice, honey mustard, sriracha, crystal hot sauce, Worcestershire sauce, Old Bay, celery seed, baking powder, parsley, and panko breadcrumbs. The sandwich uses Monterey Jack cheese and buttered bread.
dishes.guy_response: Crab cake is a little bit creamier than most people expect. Yeah. Crispy on the outside, tons of crab. No question about the amount of crab in there. Nice crunch of the toast. Not too much cheese. Pull it off, ma'am. Nicely done, bro. Thank you. Nicely done.

visits.guy_intro: So we're on the west side of Charlotte, North Carolina. Now, you remember the Penguin. Everybody remembers the Penguin. Well, it closed, but the guy, Greg, opened up a new joint over here on the west side. And you know what? This is Pinky's West Side Grill.

dishes.description: A burger with three 5-ounce patties, three pieces of American cheese, house southern slaw, mustard, chopped onions, and house-made chili.
dishes.guy_response: Greg, I'm telling you, it's fantastic. You're attacking this thing. It's like a coyote got a hold of it. Triple G, sir? It's a sloppy piece of equipment, but it's delicious and filling.

dishes.description: Shrimp dipped in a sweet, crunchy corn dog batter and deep-fried.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, yeah. They're so good. It tastes like a corn dog. Sweet, crunchy batter. Straight from the fair. Straight from the fair. Straight from the fair. Not salty.

name: Hanalei Dolphin
owner_chef: Douglas and Beatrice Allen, Chef Nephi Takenaka

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: If you've been coming to the island of Kauai since the 1970s, you've probably heard of this place. Or have a buddy that's worked at this place, or know people that are huge fans of this place, I mean, it's literally an institution on the island. Now, it's changed hands quite a few times. And the current owners have worked here since 95, bought it in 2007. This is the Hanalei dolphin.

dishes.description: Sushi-grade ahi tuna marinated in a teriyaki sauce and grilled medium-rare, served with rice and drawn butter.
dishes.guy_response: When I got into this, it is perfectly seared. It is literally cold in the center. Exactly how I like it. Little bit of rice to it. And the butter has that little kick of salt. Oh, yeah, absolutely. A little bit of the fat. That is the way to have it right there, my friend. You weren't lying on that one. I wouldn't have done it. Glad you liked it. Here's the thing that I'm happiest about. You didn't take that delicious piece of ahi and hammer the marinade all the way through it. Without question, you really get to taste the fish. I can see why this has been on the menu that long. Well done.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: It's not just about the variations. It's about the freshness. That's why I'm here in Kaneohe, about 11 miles from Honolulu. This is Fresh Catch.

dishes.description: Small cuts of Aji tuna seasoned with Hawaiian sea salt, kukui nut, Hawaiian chili flakes, Hawaiian chili pepper water, sesame oil, green onion, white onion, and limu (seaweed).
dishes.guy_response: I could sit here and eat this by the bucket full. It's tender. It's fresh. All the ingredients go together well. It's so balanced. It's so delicate. It's so flavorful. You just get the real deal in this, man. I've got a lot of poke on this island as well. That's legit. Thank you very much, my man.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Nothing better than cruising around the island of Kauai with my right-hand man, Hunter. Hunter, what are we hungry for? Poke. Exactly. Now, Dad, how do you find the best poke? What do you look for? Well, I mean, there's places all over. But I got to tell you, you talk to the locals, you look around. And when you pull into a parking lot and you see a line like that, you got to check it out. This is Hanalei Poke.

dishes.description: A poke bowl with sushi rice, fresh ahi, fresh ono, green onion, fresh limu (seaweed), red onion, mango, watermelon, local avocados, fresh mint, basil, cilantro, macadamia nuts, inamona (roasted kukui and red pepper), sesame oil sriracha mix, and chili pepper shoyu.
dishes.guy_response: I've tried all kinds of flavors I've never had in a poke bowl. The mint, the watermelon. That watermelon is so good in there. That is so refreshing. Really nice. The kukui with the mint, game changer. You've got creamy, spicy, salty, nutty, crunchy. The macadamia. The macadamia nut. It makes the salad even better. It really does. It does. That's delicious right there.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in the southwest area of Portland, Oregon, to check out a place where the chef came from a long background in the corporate world. And finally, one day, he said he's going to break free. That's right. He's going to do things on his own terms. Do things his own way. He's going to cook from his favorite seasons and his favorite regions. Wait a second. That'd be a perfect name for a restaurant. This is Seasons and Regions Seafood Grill.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos on the West Coast with Guy Fieri | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in the southwest area of Portland, Oregon, to check out a place where the chef came from a long background in the corporate world. And finally, one day, he said he's going to break free. That's right, he's going to do things on his own terms, do things his own way. He's going to cook from his favorite seasons and his favorite regions. Wait a second, that'd be a perfect name for a restaurant. This is Seasons and Regions Seafood Grill.

dishes.dish_name: Northwest Seafood Cioppino with Dungeness Crab Cake
dishes.description: A cioppino broth with salmon, rockfish, and salt spring mussels, topped with a deep-fried Dungeness crab cake.
dishes.guy_response: That's really good. That broth is fantastic. Typically, when you have cioppino, you know, you like to have some bread or something to dip it into. And this crab cake on top is perfectly crunchy, which would be kind of like the crusty bread going into it. Works incredibly well. Thank you, chef, for wrecking it. Because this is what I'm going to expect now is a little salty, crunchy crab right on top. Something I didn't see coming, but I definitely would eat all day for the rest of the week.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in Juneau, Alaska, and every other day, thousands of people get off those cruise ships, and they walk right by this funky little joint where the chef, who's worked through the ranks and studied a little bit of Italy, has opened up a joint where he's scratch-making, hand-making just about everything on the menu. It's Alaskan food with an Italian influence. This is in Boca a Lupo.

dishes.description: Handmade pappardelle noodles served with Alaskan king crab and a seafood bisque sauce, finished with lemon creme fraiche and sorrel.
ingredients: pappardelle (low-gluten flour, egg yolks, olive oil, sea salt)
dishes.guy_response: Delicious. The bisque is really nice and light and refreshing. The crab is not tough. The pasta is the heart of the dish. It is super tender. And the key is the creme fraiche. Two of my favorite things, crab bisque and a pasta dish. Outstanding.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in Eugene, Oregon, home of the Ducks, University of Oregon. Now, what else can you expect when you come to Eugene an hour and a half off the coast of Oregon? Exactly. Great fresh seafood. That's because they got this joint. Fisherman's Market.

dishes.description: Fresh Dungeness crab cooked in a crab pot, served with lemon juice, butter, fries, and slaw.
dishes.guy_response: But that is hot crab. That's outrageous. Without question, the most seafood-flavored crab really, I think, has to be the Dungeness. Some lemon juice. I'll take a little dip in some butter. That slaw, nice crunch, really refreshes the palate. That's the freshest experience you can get. It's juicy. It's succulent. You don't even need the butter. It's so wonderful.

dishes.description: A pie with a great crust, filled with tender and succulent crawfish, served with sour cream on top.
dishes.guy_response: Not soupy. Not too thick. Tender. There is tender and succulent and the texture of it is awesome. I didn't know about the sour cream thing you were doing but it's excellent.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: What do you get when you combine a marine biologist and a chef? No, this is not a joke, you guys. I'm really serious about this. You get a funky little joint right here on the main street of Vancouver, Canada that's serving up healthy and sustainably harvested seafood. This is the fish counter.

dishes.description: Fish and chips made with Harrison River Wild Keta salmon, battered and fried. Served with fresh-cut Kennebec fries, tartar sauce, and slaw.
dishes.guy_response: that's got a good amount of flavor. Not oily one bit. And you get some great crunch. Batter's not too much. It's not competing with the fish. Carter sauce. That's a nice balance right there. Fries are legit. Give me the rundown on the slaw. Kale, cabbage, rice vinegar, and celery seeds. Nice juxtaposition to the salty fried crunchy. Nicely done, my friend.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, and I got this buddy, and he says next time you come into town... Hey, man, check this out. Anyhow, he says next time you come into town, you gotta find a great seafood restaurant because I'm a, uh, what are you again? I'm a vegiquarium. It means the only vegetables in seafood. Ladies and gentlemen, the one and only Vanilla Ice. Dude, what are you, stuck in the 90s? The best decade ever, man. This is Pirate Republic.

dishes.description: A Brazilian seafood stew with golden corvina, shrimp, clams, calamari, and mussels, cooked in a sauce made with blended oil, garlic, onion, bay leaf, salt, pepper, house-made pepper sauce, bell peppers, pureed tomatoes, dende oil (palm tree oil), and coconut milk.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Isn't that dynamite? Everything is cooked right, not overcooked. I love the kick. That is really good. Tastes a little Creole almost. It's spicy. It's tangy. The kicker to the whole thing is the coconut milk. That's what gives it that luxurious, that creamy, that silkiness. It's fantastic.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: But with a name like Joe's Gizzard City, you know why I'm here. The gizzard's up. And they're the best gizzards in the world. Thank goodness we're serving them from chickens. That great idea came from Joe Sr., who took over his father's chicken joint in 1968.

dishes.description: Gizzards pressure cooked to tenderize, seasoned with garlic powder, celery salt, and salt, then battered with a special flour mix and deep-fried.
dishes.guy_response: I don't think, I think they're quite tasty, actually. I think the seasoning that you pressure cook them in, that makes a big difference. The batter is killer. Don't get gizzards anywhere else. Who needs gizzards? Gizzards are the best.

dishes.description: Chicken boiled first to ensure juiciness, then battered with garlic powder and celery salt, and deep-fried.
dishes.guy_response: It's super tender and moist. My gosh, it falls apart. You know the first thing I get out of that? What's that? It's that garlic flavor of that chicken when it boiled. Mm. Sure is a different way to do chicken.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Fried chicken is coming up. It's like Easter. It's made in Southwestern Salt Lake City. It's a flavor bomb. Like the Pakistani flavors Sonny Nassar learned to cook with at his parents' popular joint down the street.

dishes.description: Chicken brined for 24 hours in a mixture of water, vinegar, lemon juice, black pepper, salt, curry powder, house garam masala, ginger, and garlic. Then coated in house spice and deep-fried for 15 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: The fried chicken. That's good crisp on that. I like to get the pita bread. Put everything in it and just make a little bite of it. It's good stuff. Mmm. To get all that curry spice, then you get the moisture and the fat of the chicken. Wow, that's a lot of flavor. Bro, real deal. Thank you. This chicken is unique. It wrecks fried chicken. There's so much flavor in it. It wrecks it. It's very crunchy, very juicy.

dishes.description: Yellow and green lentils cooked for about two hours with salt, garam masala, hot curry powder, crushed chili peppers, and Indian chili powder. Topped with sizzled onions and cumin.
dishes.guy_response: If all you sold was spicy lentils, I would show up. That's it. The lentils are really gangster.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: These pizzas are the creation of chef and owner Tom Faye, who came back home after culinary school and opened this joint in 2011. Knowing that we needed to fill a niche in town, serve pizza by the slice and just have it. No one was doing pizza by the slice? No. Just needed to be up to par. We needed some good pizza in town.

dishes.description: Starts with chicken breast marinated for 24 hours in basil, garlic, lemon slices, and oil, then roasted. Built on hand-tossed dough with fresh basil pesto, mozzarella cheese, roasted chicken, artichoke hearts, red onions, and ricotta cheese, then baked at 575 degrees for 10 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: Roasting the chicken with the garlic. Outrageous. Making your own pesto over the top. Little bit of that ricotta coming in. Creaminess, saltiness.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: This marine right here, this aviator, has got his hands full with a scratch-made joint and a really cool sports bar. You named the joint Sidewinders after something that was so major in your life, being a pilot in the Marine Corps. So I could understand why, when you're going to do something to that degree, you're going to make sure that the food has that much respect as well. And you really have shown that. That's not typically what you find in a sports bar.

visits.video_title: Top 20 #DDD Videos in the Rockies with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Welcome back. Having a great time hanging out at Sidewinder's in Jackson, Wyoming. This Marine right here, this aviator has got his hands full with this. With a scratch-made joint and a really cool sports bar.

dishes.description: Roasted chicken (rubbed with thyme, oregano, parsley, celery salt, mustard, bushberry pepper, cayenne, red chili flake, and granulated garlic) combined with a filling of mirepoix (white onions, garlic, celery, carrot) sweated down, deglazed with white wine, then mushrooms, salt, pepper, basil, oregano, parsley, cayenne, chicken stock, 'cowboy roux', heavy cream, and potatoes. Topped with puff pastry and egg wash, then baked.
dishes.guy_response: Good looking pot pie. You know, there's something about chicken on the bone, to make the stock from the bone. There's such a depth. It's creamy. Love that you got the mushrooms in there. Not a ton of crust. The veggies are tender. But you get some real texture out of those carrots and the celery. Dynamite.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I got to see some chicken and dumplings. So how do we make it here? We bake the chickens for Wednesday. We have baked chicken on the menu. My dad wanted to incorporate leftovers into an entree, not to waste the food. So he started doing chicken and dumplings.

dishes.description: Baked chicken (seasoned with salt, black pepper, chili powder, brown sugar, and paprika) is shredded. Gravy is built from margarine, celery, onion, garlic, chicken drippings, water, black pepper, white pepper, poultry seasoning, sage, and chicken base, then fortified with pulled chicken. Dumplings are made from all-purpose and self-rising flour, salt, lard, and ice water, then rolled out and cut. Cooked together until thickened.
dishes.guy_response: Creaminess. Tender chunks of chicken. I almost finished the whole bowl in that time.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Cheers. This is the neighborhood pub with above average pub fare. It's off the beaten path, but the food is really great. You come for the food. I come for the food. And the one two keg kitchen punch was all part of the plan when John Raleigh and Jeffrey Kaplan partnered up on this place. I wanted to do like a gastropub with good beer and he wanted a farmhouse brewery with good food. We do like farm to table dishes we all grew up with, but just a little upscale.

dishes.description: Chicken (airliner cut) brined overnight in hot water, garlic powder, kosher salt, and sugar, then sous vide at 165F for an hour, then dredged in rice flour and fried. Biscuit made from flour, salt, baking soda, baking powder, cold butter, local goat cheddar, green onion, and buttermilk. Assembled with horseradish crema, fried chicken, house-made pickles, and onion rings.
dishes.guy_response: Nice, tender biscuit. Great chicken. This is tender. Great flavor. You're definitely getting a little bit of the brine. It's a light, crisp fry. And the brine of the pickle and the brine. The brine of the pickle and the horseradish sauce is dynamite. I give you props.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: And you can get some really good barbecue. But this neighborhood joint wasn't initially on chef and owner Dan Sheridan's radar. I had a small pickling company going on. Pickling company. Yeah. Everybody has one of those. We would have to sell tons of pickles to pay rent. I mean, that'll put you in a pickle. So Dan tapped into his culinary background and went full-blown barbecue. Smoking up everything from beef. Brisket rib plate. To bird. Like what this little barbecue spot is known for. Chicken salad sandwich.

dishes.description: A 3.5lb chicken spatchcocked, rubbed with a dry rub (cumin, garlic powder, onion powder, chili powder, cheese, salt, cayenne, black pepper, dry mustard, Cajun seasoning, paprika, brown sugar), smoked for 2.5-3 hours over cherry wood at 250F, then shredded. Mixed with mayo, white and dark meat, celery, house-made bacon bits, lemon, dill, seasoning salt, salt, and pepper. Served on brioche buns with house-made pickles.
dishes.guy_response: Any time you're making smoked chicken with smoked bacon, put them together in a sandwich. Can't go wrong. Not too much smoke on the chicken. We get a little celery in there. Base foundation. All right. What are these pickled with? Vinegar, salt, sugar. There's some dill in there, our dry rub, and some Old Bay. The dry rub gets in the pickles, too? Yes, sir. Everything. Well done. Congratulations.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: 50s diner kind of place. It's like a down-home country kitchen. Biscuits and gravy up. You get a lot. A lot of food. Here's your chicken fried skillet. Hungry guys to feed. A lot of lagers, construction guys. And just about everyone else. We come here all the time, and we love it. Pancakes, hashbrowns, everything. Simple, straightforward, comfort food, man. Same stuff. This joint's been serving up for decades. So when long-time restaurant vet Jimmy Berti bought the place. He kept what worked. It's essentially the same menu. With a few of his own twists.

dishes.description: Chicken (skinned, rubbed with olive oil and rosemary) baked at 350F for 40 minutes, then deboned. Dough for dumplings made from flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, butter, and buttermilk, rolled out and cut with a shot glass. Dumplings baked separately for 6 minutes. Broth made from water, carrots, dill, parsley, basil, and salt, then onions, celery, peas, and chicken are added and boiled for 10 minutes. Served with small biscuits.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. I didn't think it was gonna nearly have the chicken flavor. You got a ton in there. Look at that big piece of chicken. The veggies. Mmm. Look at the dumplings, though. Right there. Firm, but tender dumplings. Got texture. Mmm. Delicious, man. Scratch-made chicken and dumplings. You don't see this every day. It's good eats, man. Mmm.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: ...the name is still Uncle Lou's Fried Chicken, and he's taking it to the max. Whole fried chicken? That's our signature item.

dishes.description: Whole chicken injected with Cajun Creole butter, then deep-fried. Legs and thighs are cooked first for 40 minutes. The fried chicken is then dunked in 'the corruption' sauce, a sweet and spicy glaze.
dishes.guy_response: Wow. Oh, my gosh. You could really pick up the flavor of that garlic butter. You really don't need a knife. Look how tender and juicy that is. That's it. It's not really oily. I think people think, oh, you fried it. It's gonna be all oily. Take our first family photo. Looks like us, doesn't it. You're off the chain, man. I got to tell you. Wow. That's good. This is like real chicken.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Barbecue nachos with pork. Food here is phenomenal. Half rack, bean and mac. It is a bonanza of barbecue bliss. Courtesy of champion barbecuers, Darren Wark and Shad Curtin, two dudes who opened this joint in 2010. And at 11,000 square feet, well, I'm calling it the Swine Coliseum. This is nuts. Three full-blown smokers, 24-7. That's a big Ferris wheel in Flavortown. The brisket, pork, the ribs are crazy. And don't forget about the chicken.

dishes.description: Chicken wings are smoked, then deep-fried, and finally glazed with an Asian sauce.
dishes.guy_response: And I like that you cook it when you're crispy. It's fantastic. Cherrywood plays in perfect. Great spice, nice balance, subtle, not overdone. Great job, brother.

dishes.description: A dessert featuring apples baked inside dough, cooked in a syrup. Described as having a balance of tang, crunch, and soft dough.
dishes.guy_response: This is money. Well, you get that tang. You get a little bit of the crunch of the dough. Then you get some of the soft dough that's cooked in the syrup the entire time. Apple dumpling gang rides again. This rocks.

dishes.description: Chicken wings rubbed with oil, smoked for 45 minutes at 275F, then deep-fried. Tossed in a dry rub (half Cajun seasoning, half rib rub), moonshine barbecue sauce, cheese, and bacon, then baked. Topped with diced tomato, green onion, spicy ranch, and sour cream.
dishes.guy_response: I love the smoke, a little fry to it. We need the tortilla chip. You get the crunch of the outside of the wing. The cheese melts on really nice. Then you have the bacon sticking to it. So you get all best of both worlds all over it. You hit that moonshine sauce on there, which is dynamite. You get yourself some of the cool sour cream. That's one of the most unique dishes I've seen on Triple D. And you actually pull it off.

visits.video_title: Top Chicken Dishes on #DDD with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: We're talking about scratch made sandwiches, funky creations. Nacho pollo loco in the window. The nacho pollo loco sandwich is ridiculous. The chicken itself is breaded with the nacho cheese chips. You don't find this anywhere else. It's really delicious. Now we're going to get into a chicken sandwich that's coated in nacho cheese chips. This is the kind of stuff that we used to make up when we were in college. When you would have the leftover potato chips that were the crumbles at the bottom, I would always incorporate those. Just when you think they weren't going to let you be healthy at the Full Belly Deli, they go ahead and bake the chicken.

dishes.description: Chicken breast dredged in seasoned egg wash (hot sauce, kosher salt, black pepper), then packed with nacho cheese chips and baked. Served on jalapeno cheddar bread with Southwest sauce (chipotles in adobo, cilantro, mayonnaise, sour cream, horseradish, lemon juice, salt, pepper), romaine lettuce, heirloom tomatoes, and house-made pickles (cucumbers sous vide in white vinegar, sugar, salt, yellow mustard seed, caraway seed, peppercorns, garlic, bay leaf, arbol chili, and dill).
dishes.guy_response: It's got good texture, good chew, but not heavy. I love the cheese and the jalapenos on the outside. And the chicken breast, it's got a really good crunch. dishes.description: Pancake batter with oatmeal, banana, and walnuts, cooked on a griddle to achieve a nice crunchy outside.

dishes.description: Brioche bread dipped in an egg, milk, and vanilla mixture, coated in crushed cornflakes, grilled, then filled with whipped cream cheese and raspberry jelly, and drizzled with more raspberry jelly.

visits.video_title: Top 10 #DDD Meatball Videos of All Time with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Cleveland, Ohio. And it's been a while since I've visited. Let me tell you, my cousins remind me of it all the time. You know, the food scene is really blown up here. And with so much culture, when I hear that there's a Greek joint where they're importing their own goods, you know I got to check it out. This is Astoria Cafe and Market. Astoria is probably the most authentic Mediterranean restaurant in Cleveland. You can go all out. You can get a four-course meal. Or you can just come grab a beer and a pizza. It's a place to get fine imported wine and cheeses. Which is how cousins George Consios and Steve Daniels first rolled out, leaning into their Greek heritage in the import business. We started literally as a wholesale. And wanted to do primarily Mediterranean products. This is awesome. Beautiful market. Love the deli case. And when they expanded into a full-blown restaurant, they brought in chefs. Corey Korinski to keep the Mediterranean vibe alive.

All the stuff comes right from the garden outside. Oh, that's great. Isn't that amazing? I love that. What's amazing is you. Absolutely fabulous. I haven't had one item here that I haven't liked.

You're getting the volume of a burger, the flavor of the bacon, the whole top of that patty is just all that crispy, crunchy, unctuous flavor. Then on the inside, it's super tender. dishes.description: A BLT featuring a 9oz patty made from ground applewood smoked pork belly (bacon) and lean meat, cooked in a wood-fired oven. Served on house-made potato bread (kaiser roll) with mayonnaise, heirloom tomatoes, and organic greens, accompanied by house-made onion rings.
dishes.guy_response: Bro, this is what everybody wants a BLT to be like. You're getting the volume of a burger, the flavor of the bacon, the whole top. A roux is built with flour and chicken stock, finished with whole milk. Layered with tortillas, pulled dark and white chicken, Monterey Jack and cheddar cheese, and sauce. dishes.guy_response: So it's green chilies. They come through creamy, not spicy, which gets misinterpreted a lot of times when you talk about Tex-Mex food. Just like Matthew was saying, having that dark meat, I think you did the right balance. Yeah, real good consistency, man. Not too wet, not too dry. Shuffle down a plateful of that? Easily. Cachelata. That's like Texas comfort food.

visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Logan, Utah, about an hour and a half outside of Salt Lake City. But these days, I'm looking for Indian food. Oh, you're seeing it sprout up all over the country. Big flavors, great stories. And I got one for you right here at a gas station. This is Tandoori Oven.

dishes.description: Chicken marinated for 48 hours in homemade yogurt, salt, paprika, turmeric, garam masala, lemon juice, ginger garlic, and food coloring, then cooked in a tandoor oven at 400F with mesquite charcoal. Sauce made from onion gravy (oil, cumin, coriander, onions, tomato, water) and a separate sauce (oil, ginger garlic, garam masala, turmeric, paprika, added to onion/tomato base, blended). Chicken is added to sauce with ginger garlic, bell pepper, cream, and simmered until boiled. Served with rice (cloves, bay leaf, cumin seed) and garnished with fenugreek leaf and cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, so good. This is very approachable. That's probably one of the staple dishes that people should try when they're trying Indian food for the first time. Oh, my gosh. I'm telling you. Ah. Mm. Thank you.

visits.video_title: Top 30 #DDD Videos of ALL TIME with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: All right, ho dad, this is the joint. How's the food? Food's amazing. And the story is even better. That's my kind of joint. This is the OB Noodle House.

dishes.description: Mid-thigh chicken wings seasoned with garlic, salt, pepper, sugar, and marinated for a day in chili oil satay sauce (hot oil, ground ginger, garlic, brown lemongrass, ground onions, stirred for 40 mins, chili paste, chili flake, Hoppin' Arrow peppers, Thai chili pepper, peanut butter, hoisin sauce, simmered for an hour, then rested for a day). Wings are fried at 350F, then wok-tossed with hot oil, garlic, pepper, seasoning (sugar, granulated garlic, white pepper), and green onions. Garnished with cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, my gosh. Yeah. A little bit of heat, not too much. Good amount of spice to it. A little bit of sweet. Just a little bit of the crispiness on the skin. They're tender. They're juicy. There's a lot of flavor, a lot of depth of flavor. I'm impressed.

visits.video_title: Top 30 #DDD Videos of ALL TIME with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: You know, I love being from California. We got some of the best of the best. But what I wish we had more of is old school Italian delis like this, where they're scratch making their own desserts. They got a deli case full of everything. We're talking about prepared meals. We're talking fresh pasta, homemade sausage. I mean, you name it, they got it. So the next time you find yourself in Robbinsville, New Jersey, you got to check out Dolce and Clemente's.

dishes.description: House-made high-gluten pizza dough (water, yeast, salt, sugar, oil, mixed 10-12 mins, rested overnight, folded and kneaded, rolled) filled with prosciutto, ham, mortadella, super salsa (grandfather's recipe), pepperoni, provolone cheese, and crushed black pepper. Baked at 350F for 38 minutes. Served with extra virgin olive oil and locatelli cheese, garnished with parsley.
dishes.guy_response: Ridiculous. When you cook these Italian meats and you let all the fat cook into the dough, you just get all the flavor. The depth of flavor in this is massive. The key, though, is how you cook the meat on the outside of the bread. Gives it that crunch. Out of bounds. Seriously, out of bounds.

visits.video_title: Top 30 #DDD Videos of ALL TIME with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Burlington, Vermont, to check out a joint. Where this guy's getting such acclaim, people are saying that he should be the driver of the vegan bust of Flavortown. This is Pingala.

dishes.description: Flour tortilla filled with oat cheese (rolled oats, nutritional yeast, turmeric, garlic powder, salt, lemon juice, oil, tapioca flour, boiled), brown rice, chili (sunflower seed oil, fresh garlic, mirepoix, serranos, chili powder, cumin, paprika, cinnamon, salt, brown sugar, tomatoes, organic tomato paste, tamari, lentils, black beans, cooked 1-1.5 hrs), cashew cheese (soaked cashews, sweet potato puree, nutritional yeast, cayenne, oregano, garlic powder, turmeric, salt, pepper, lemon juice, hot water), crunchy tortillas, not avocado mash (chickpeas, peas, nutritional yeast, lemon extract, herb oil - fresh garlic, raw onion, rosemary, sage, tarragon, parsley, salt, oil), tomato, scallion, cilantro, and pickled jalapeno. Folded and pressed on a panini press.
dishes.guy_response: It's outstanding. The textures. The seasoning. The fake avocado. Yeah. Probably was the foundation of me getting my mind blown. I mean, out of vegetarian, vegan dishes I've had in my life, this is easily in the top five. If not top three. I would challenge anybody who doesn't think they like vegetarian vegan to come down here and try this. It's remarkable. Get on a plane and come to Vermont, remarkable.

visits.video_title: Top 30 #DDD Videos of ALL TIME with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: On Triple D, when we hit an historic place like Baltimore, well, we got to use the inside track of a buddy who lives here. So regardless of the weather or how busy this dude is, we're at Charm City Cakes to talk to the cake bad boy, Duff Goldman, and find out one of his favorite joints. I think probably Chaps. Chaps. Let's get some pit beef.

dishes.description: Bottom round beef cooked on a charcoal pit grill at 400-500F, pulled at desired doneness (medium rare to well done), thinly sliced, and piled high on a roll. Served with white onions, salt, pepper, and horseradish.
dishes.guy_response: You know, I like the little char to it. But I gotta say, the medley of the white onion is the best. The really nice, bright horseradish, a little bit of salt and pepper, a great roll. Not too much bread. I hate when you get too much bread and it starts to become... It's all about the meat. Wow. Mm. That was all horseradish. Good for you. Whoa. There it is. My toes are tingling. I mean, seriously. I go like, wow. Welcome to Baltimore. It's spicy. It's moist. The meat is so tender. I give it way past a 10.

dishes.dish_name: The Bulldog (Pit Beef and Sausage Combo)
dishes.description: A combo sandwich with pit beef and sausage on a roll, with cheese.
dishes.guy_response: I didn't think the pit beef would get any better. Got a little sausage kicker. Mm.

dishes.dish_name: Custom Combo (Pit Beef, Ham, Corned Beef)
dishes.description: A large sandwich with pit beef, ham, and corned beef on a roll.
dishes.guy_response: Is there anything wrong with this picture? Actually, no. There's not. When the bread won't even wrap around, that is so much flavor. Sweetness of the ham, that briny, pickly spice on the corned beef, and then just a nice little bit of the sauce. It's just so good. It's so good. It's so good. It's so good. It's so good. And then just a nice, smoky flavor from the pit beef. The roll that can withstand the pressure. This is like sandwich engineering right here. Good mouthful. It is. Good mouthful.

dishes.description: A massive custom-built sandwich for Duff Goldman, starting with pit beef, jalapenos, horseradish, ham, a burger patty (cut in half), nacho cheese sauce, sausage, pickles, chopped beef, and turkey.

visits.video_title: Top 30 #DDD Videos of ALL TIME with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I heard about this family that started off in the fair business, and then they went and opened a restaurant. This is Grammy's Goodies.

dishes.description: Layers of noodles, grandmother's sundae sauce (olive oil, pork ribs, prosciutto, garlic salt, onion, parsley, fresh tomatoes, fresh basil, black pepper, salt, tomato paste, water, cooked 3 hours), ricotta cheese mixture (whole milk, ricotta cheese, black pepper, eggs, fresh parsley, mozzarella provolone blend, romano cheese), Nana's meatballs (half pork, half ground beef, chopped onions, soft bread crumbs, basil, garlic, parsley, romano cheese, salt, black pepper, eggs, pan-fried then baked 15 mins at 350F), and sausage (family recipe, 100 years old). Baked for about 3 hours.
dishes.guy_response: So I know that this is the lasagna portion for the entire family. Lots of ricotta, delicious meatballs, tender pasta, dynamite sauce. This is old school, American Italian, exactly the kind of lasagna I would expect to go and get at Grammy's house. Dynamite.

visits.video_title: Top 30 #DDD Videos of ALL TIME with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: It's been about 12 years since I've had this place since we first came to OKC. I can't figure out how I missed a joint that's been doing it for 67 years. They've been doing this country cooking, and they're getting known now for something called yam fried chicken. Oh, that's right up my alley. This is Florence's Restaurant.

dishes.description: Spare ribs or St. Louis-style ribs cut, brined in lemon juice and vinegar water for a day or longer. Seasoned with salt and pepper, then dredged in seasoned flour (salt, corn starch, baking powder, pepper). Fried for 15 minutes. Served with house-made barbecue sauce (water, sugar, onion powder, barbecue spice, cumin, mustard, liquid smoke, vinegar, ketchup, cooked down).
dishes.guy_response: That's tasty. Not too much batter. Nice and juicy. I think the key was that you soaked them in the vinegar. Got a nice little flavor from that. Oh, there's good tang in that sauce, ma'am. Thank you. I'm gonna have more fried ribs.

dishes.description: Chicken seasoned with salt and pepper, then dredged in seasoned flour. Fried in Dutch ovens. Served with candy yams (cooked yams, sugar, butter, cooked until syrupy) and collard greens (cooked with water, baking soda, sugar, salt, chicken stock for 1.5 hours).
dishes.guy_response: Delicious. You really seasoned your chicken well. There's a lot of flavor in that pot. I love collard greens. Nice and tender, not mushy. Perfectly cooked chicken, tender candied yams, and you. Package deal. Fantastic. I need that yam fried chicken. Amazing. Tender and juicy on the inside. The yam sauce just made it so good. Made it so delicious.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Korean-Inspired Food Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in the West Midtown area of Atlanta. I'm here to check out a joint that's doing tacos. Well, they're not really even tacos. They're doing Korean food. Well, it's not even really Korean, it's more like Korean tacos. It's unconventional flavor fusion that's been on my radar since 2018. And these days, there's a whole lot more of them. This is Hanguk Ta Korea.

dishes.description: Chicken tenders brined in buttermilk, double-dredged in all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, pepper mix, then fried. Served on warm tortillas with chipotle aioli, soy sesame salad, and jack cheese.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, great crunch from the chicken. It's delicious. Korean fried chicken is ready. When you bite into it, it's got that Korean flavor, and it has a little bit of a spice.

dishes.description: Shrimp dredged in all-purpose flour, rice flour, garlic powder, onion powder, sea salt, fresh cracked pepper, water, then fried. Served on tortillas with sole sauce, hoisin tartar, and soy sesame salad.
dishes.guy_response: That's outstanding. I give up. I'm waving the flag, bro. That might be one of my favorites. Appreciate that. When I bite into that, I'm just like, yeah, that's it. I think it's the balance of flavors, don't you? This makes the average taco very boring.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Korean-Inspired Food Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I love Colorado. I mean, they got so much going on here, especially the food scene. I mean, who could forget Fat Sully's? Give me some pie. Then you got Lauer Kraut's. Love that place. So there's no doubt that these guys got a Korean restaurant. And not just any Korean restaurant. This one's off the hook. This is Deji Korean Barbecue.

dishes.description: Thinly sliced ribeye marinated for 12 hours in a sauce made with water, garlic, onions, pineapples, apples, soy sauce, sugar, black pepper, sesame oil, and soju. Cooked on a grill.
Chicken marinated for 24 hours in garlic, soybean oil, sesame oil, ginger, black pepper, sugar, soy sauce, and soju. Cooked on a grill.

Pancakes made with fermented kimchi (cabbage, onions, garlic, shrimp, daikon radish, ginger, rice, seafood broth, fish sauce, Korean red peppers, sugar), tofu, jalapenos, onions, sugar, Korean red peppers, egg, potato starch, and flour. Fried/sauteed.
Fish cakes cooked in a wok with onions, green onions, carrots, jalapenos, garlic, soy sauce, black pepper, sugar, corn syrup, seafood broth, crushed red peppers, Korean red peppers, and sesame oil.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Butter Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in Logan, Utah. I found a place that's still rockin' it. This is Maymo's.

dishes.description: House-made 81-19 burger patty with brisket fat, topped with smoked bone marrow butter, pepper jack cheese, horseradish sauce, lettuce, tomato, and pickle, served on a garlic butter potato bun.
dishes.guy_response: You execute in the finer parts. The bun's great. The little brush of the garlic butter's dynamite. The bone marrow butter is, of course, unctuous. You can't mistake the richness of bone marrow. If you're gonna eat a burger and you're gonna eat it at lunch and you're gonna go pass out in your car, that's the burger you want. Well done.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Butter Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Chef and owner Julius Thompson had to travel a tough road before finding reward in this culinary gem. This is Sauce Boss Southern Kitchen.

dishes.description: Cheesy grits cooked with butter and chicken base, topped with devained peeled shrimp and bacon cooked in a blackening butter made with garlic and a blend of spices.
dishes.guy_response: Grits are cooked right. Got good tooth. You can put a lot of cheese in there, but I mean, I don't know you can really have to when it's cheese and shrimp and grits. The lardone from the bacon is the little gem of the shrimp. The shrimp's cooked perfectly, not super spicy, but a lot of big flavors that you're putting in there. Well done, Chef.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Butter Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in Boulder, Colorado, in the downtown area, about a five minute walk north of Colorado University. Now, Triple D, we've checked out joints for tortillas, pizza, pasta, chicken wings, cheese steaks, I mean, you name it. But we've not yet found a joint that's been rocking the crepe. That's until now. This is Foolish Craig's.

dishes.description: Layers of thin crepes made with eggs, milk, sugar, salt, butter, and flour, interleaved with a sauce of butter, brown sugar, cream, and rum, then frosted with a cream cheese, butter, vanilla, and powdered sugar frosting.
dishes.guy_response: The texture on that is out of bounds. The sweetness is not over the top. Using the cream cheese to make the frosting helps out a lot. I've never seen this before. It's a great recipe. Hey, call the patent office right now, dude.

dishes.description: A Greek chicken pie with chicken breast cooked with dill, onion, garlic, carrots, and celery, mixed with parmesan, feta, bechamel sauce, and eggs, all wrapped in buttered phyllo dough and baked.
dishes.guy_response: That thing is crunch-a-licious. Wow, that's not creamy. This is like a pasty, but done with the phyllo dough. The layers. That's crazy. It was great. That's everywhere in Greece. What? Everywhere. It's in the bakery like a donut. In the morning? Yeah. Oh, see? That's my kind of breakfast. Knocked it out with the phyllo.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Butter Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in New Orleans on Broad Street, just a few blocks away from Canal that'll take you right down to the corner. Listen, I love coming to this city. You got great people, amazing food, all kinds of characters. You got the party. You got the history. And listen, I'm here to check out a joint that's got all that under one roof. This is Margie's Grill.

dishes.description: Spatchcocked chicken rubbed with a spice blend including Japanese and Vietnamese chilies, smoked over oak logs, then glazed with honey and butter, and served with a chili garlic sauce made with shallots, lime juice, fish sauce, and cane sugar.
dishes.guy_response: I'll tell you what, that's good eating. I got a sweet glaze and smoke taking me into great Southern chicken. And then all of a sudden, I come into fish sauce and chilies. Yeah, and I mean, I think that it bridges the Louisiana influence and the Southeast Asian influence. I don't know how much it bridges it as much as it creates its own new superhighway. This garlic sauce should be bottled. This chicken, you get the heat, you get the sweet, you get the vinegar, you get the spice, you get the salt. You're setting people up to be disappointed next time they have to eat just regular smoked chicken. Dude, that's righteous.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Butter Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in the Montrose area of Houston, Texas. And man, I don't know if you've been down here before, but these folks have got a bar for just about anything. You've got every occasion. You've got honky tonks, you've got sports bars, you've got karaoke bars. And I hear about this beach bar where they've got dynamite drinks and a chef-driven menu. You know I'm going to check it out. This is the toasted coconut.

dishes.description: Fried chicken brined in pickle brine, dredged in a seasoned flour blend with gochugaru and bullet chilies, served on house-made biscuits with American cheese and a spicy honey butter crisp made with mushroom powder, chili crisp, and pickled jalapeno liquid.
dishes.guy_response: Dynamite. The chicken is delicious. Great crunch. The honey butter crisp is redonk-a-donk. Love a savory biscuit. Who put that much black pepper in a biscuit? You better mean it. And you mean it. That's like an exceptional chicken and biscuit.

visits.video_title: A #DDD Desert Road Trip with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So here I am about 10 minutes north of downtown Albuquerque, New Mexico, right off the I-25, in a big industrial area that's packed with... Wait for it. No, wait for it. Exactly what you're thinking. Micro breweries. A bunch of them. But we're here to see one in particular. This joint. Nexus Brewery and Restaurant.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Fried Food Videos with Guy Fieri (Part 2) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So here I am about 10 minutes north of downtown Albuquerque, New Mexico, right off the I-25. We're here to see one in particular, this joint. Nexus Brewery and Restaurant.

dishes.description: Chicken brined for 24 hours, double-dredged in seasoned flour, fried, served with collard greens, mashed potatoes, and gravy.
dishes.guy_response: Man, there's a ton of crunch. But it's not... The brine's not... The brine's not too heavy. And even though you had all that hot sauce in there, it's not spicy. It's just really flavorful. Nice mashed potatoes. Good. Crunchy chicken. A lot of flavor. And my favorite out of all of them is the greens, which I can't believe I'm finding collard greens in New Mexico. That's mandatory right there.

visits.video_title: A #DDD Desert Road Trip with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm cruising through Scottsdale, Arizona to check out a joint where, get this, the wife is a second-generation Albanian. The husband is an immigrant from Mexico. And together, they get this breakfast and lunch joint where they're scrambling... And the cops make it about 95% of the menu. This is Perk Eatery.

dishes.description: Tortillas filled with chipotle ricotta, zesty black beans, slow-roasted pork, topped with spicy New Mexico chili sauce, two eggs, and fresh cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: This is really good. The New Mexican chili enchilada sauce is right on point. A little bit of heat. Nice little sweetness to it from that honey. Call it a breakfast enchilada. Call it a breakfast burrito. Call it whatever you want. Just don't call me late for it.

visits.video_title: A #DDD Desert Road Trip with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Here in Phoenix, Arizona, love this town for Triple D. Been here a ton of times. Great chefs, great restaurants, all kind of diversity. Here to check out a restaurant that won a James Beard award back in 2012. And you know what? We need to see more of this food and more of this culture. Native American. You're going to dig it. This is the Fry Bread House.

dishes.description: Pork loin seared and added to a stew with hominy, onions, garlic, oregano, black pepper, cayenne, red chili sauce, cilantro, green onions. Served with chumath (tortilla-like bread).
dishes.guy_response: I like that. I like this scarring. That's good pozole, tender pork, nice and light, not shy on flavor at all. Good. Delicious. I love it. I'm working on the pozole. The pozole here is unique. It's a thicker stew. It has such a great flavor. The spice makes it amazing.

visits.video_title: A #DDD Desert Road Trip with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in La Quinta, which is about 30 minutes from Palm Springs, California, and I'm looking for this place. I got to tell you something, it was not easy to find, but I hear it's well worth it because if you're looking for homemade, scratch-made, real deal Mexican with a twist, they tell me this is the place Chula Artisan Eatery.

dishes.dish_name: Mom's Red Chili Chilaquiles with Pulled Pork
dishes.description: House-made corn chips topped with red chile sauce, pulled pork, green onion, queso fresco, avocado, microgreens, chile oil, and onion jam.
dishes.guy_response: The chile sauce, it's silky, a lot of chile flavor. You do this really dynamite pork, you go a little bit extra with that. The chips are nice and crunchy, the jam is just that kind of that funky little twist that makes it yours. That's the next level. Dynamite.

visits.video_title: A #DDD Desert Road Trip with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Palm Desert, California, just about 20 minutes from Palm Springs. I'm here to check out a joint where a family of four is serving up some classic and not so classic burgers. And when I was there in 2017, I got my hands on a few. Cut to now, and the family's still there. The burgers are still there. And the crowds are coming in hot. This is Gorilla Burger.

dishes.description: Burger patty with chipotle onions and peppers, chipotle ketchup, pepper jack cheese, bacon, sriracha mayo, and habanero salsa.
dishes.guy_response: You do all these little things that add to it, so you get this nice, really well-rounded heat. Mm. Oh, dynamite burger. Nice job, chef.

visits.video_title: A #DDD Desert Road Trip with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Here to check out another joint in Palm Springs. Let's see how's the story go. A guy from Guadalajara wants to have a better life, moves to the United States, gets here, finds a restaurant, buys it, gets married, has kids, brings family recipes, and he's been doing it for 37 years. That's the real deal. And this is El Marisol.

dishes.description: Chicken breast cooked in a broth, then shredded and served with a pipián sauce made from toasted guajillo, chile de arbol, california chiles, pumpkin seeds, and blue corn. Served with Mexican rice and beans.
dishes.guy_response: The freshness of the tortilla. Is really good flavor. The chicken is tender. The Diabla. Hello. The sauce is hot. And the pipián. It's got the flavor of the enchilada. But the richness of the mole. Outstanding. The rice is nice and light and flaky. The beans are creamy and silky. That's got to be something you could serve to your mom. I mean, legit. Pollo pipián right here. Pollo pipián tastes a little spicy. But really good. Their chicken is outstanding.

visits.video_title: A #DDD Desert Road Trip with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here on the California side of Route 66 just outside of Victorville, California. It is so hot. There are tons of trucks. And I am hungry. So now it's time to play the where am I going to stop and eat game. So, with my keen sense of awareness, I'm going to stop and eat where the truckers eat. Emma Jean's Hall & Burger Cafe.

dishes.description: Scratch-made biscuits with a thick sausage gravy.
dishes.guy_response: That's a real deal biscuit right there. That's tasty. I don't think I could eat more than six or 12 of these.

name: Brendan and Rachel's Wood-Fired Neapolitan Pizza Joint

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos on the West Coast with Guy Fieri | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: And for husband-wife owners, Brendan Smith and Rachel Greenspan, their wood-fired Neapolitan pizza joint, all starts with the dough. Before I got into pizza, I was really a bread baker. Neither of us is Italian. Jewish and Irish.

dishes.description: Risotto balls with cacio e pepe sauce and fresh mozzarella, breaded with oregano and black pepper, frozen, then deep-fried. Served with Calabrian chili aioli.
dishes.guy_response: There's a lot of things going on here. When you can fry it to where it tastes baked, not oily, you have a nice, light, tender, flaky, fall apart. You look at it the wrong way, it'll just crumble. You've got good sweet. And you've got great texture. And you got good salt. But it's the spice in the Calabrian chili oil that sings all the way through, mirrored with the smokiness of that paprika. Poet with a palate. Right here, brother. That's it. We are done. Arancini coming out. The outside is just crispy. It melts in your mouth. It has the peppery, cheesy flavor.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos on the West Coast with Guy Fieri | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Because John Atkinson has been at it for more than 25 years. But eight years ago, he hit a wall. I quit the restaurant business for a while, jumped on a plane to Rome, one-way ticket. No. And that was it, Italy. And that's how it started. That's how it started. When he came back, he opened his own joint with his new favorite recipe. Everything is fresh. They do it right.

dishes.dish_name: Kahlua Pig Monte Cristo Egg Rolls
dishes.description: Kahlua pig (pork butt, smoked salt, banana leaf, water, slow-cooked for 12 hours). Egg roll assembled with Swiss cheese, black forest ham, Kahlua pig, scallion, teriyaki, sesame seed. Fried. Served with wasabi mojo and teriyaki sauce.
dishes.guy_response: The Kahlua pig just really rocks and rolls. Mm. Green onion, ham, cheese, and a little bit of the wasabi mojo. I just want to hear you say money. That's like bank. Bank. I mean, there's money, and then there's bank. Kahlua pork egg roll. The sauce complements the pork. It melts in your mouth. Delicious. This place is awesome.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos on the West Coast with Guy Fieri | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I mean, who knows meat better than a butcher? And here, John and LJ Shalhoub are second and third generation. Your dad started this? Yeah, the meat company. We converted it from just meat. Just the meat company to the more restaurant side of things. And that's when Chef Cosmo joined the team.

dishes.description: Chicken thighs dry-brined and grilled. Mole sauce made with puya, guajillo, and ancho chilies, spices, tomatoes, onions, jalapenos, cilantro, garlic, almonds, and chicken stock. Served in homemade corn tortillas with pickled onions, cilantro, and queso fresco.
dishes.guy_response: Mole is dynamite. It reminds me of Christmas spice. That's a completely different mole than I've had. It's spicy, but not hot spicy. You know you're eating something different. But I'll tell you what the thing is, chef, that I really get the biggest kick out of is this corn flour tortilla. I want a quesadilla in that tortilla. Delicious. Dynamite mole, I mean, the whole thing. Well done, my friend.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos on the West Coast with Guy Fieri | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: That's because when Nadia Ajlooni took over this joint, she was looking to make everyone happy, including herself. Combining all of my interests, hospitality, cooking, business, music, bringing people together, this seemed like the perfect job. Thank you guys for coming in. I'm here during lunch, late night. It's a great place to hang out, great food.

dishes.description: Tri-tip marinated in oyster sauce, crushed garlic, black pepper, garlic salt. Nom tok sauce made with fish sauce, soy sauce, lime juice, shallots, Thai basil, mint, lime leaves, sambal, red chili flake, sugar, and rice powder.
