They'll tell you how to get here. Anyhow, you'll love this place. Tons of shops, all kinds of restaurants. Mexican, Italian, German, Nepalese, you name it. But I'm here to check out one joint in particular. It's got super yummy street food, the one and only Zo Mama.

dishes.description: Homemade steamed buns with a light, fluffy dough. The filling consists of corn pork, onion, green onions, cooking wine, salt, sugar, five spice powder, ginger, sweet bean paste, and celery. Steamed for 15 minutes and served with various dipping sauces.
dishes.guy_response: You OK? The bun's ridiculous. Ridiculous. This is light and fluffy and tender. Then this one is the? Chili paste. Like sambal? Yeah. Kryptonite. Right there. I am weak in its power. One more? OK, sure. Look at that. Oh, my gosh. Delicious. Well done.

visits.video_title: Top 20 #DDD Videos in the Rockies with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: We've got some unedited bar food at a joint that's serving up something called Barnchos. This is born in a bar.

dishes.description: Crispy homemade pub chips (brined for 24 hours) topped with shredded pork (smoked for 18 hours), bacon, hot queso (heavy cream, easy melt cheese, pepper jack, Swiss), diced red onion, jalapenos, and a spicy barbecue sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Dude, the barbecue sauce is awesome. The cheese sauce, delicious, not too much. Good smoke on the pork. Love the big chunks of bacon. I thought that I was going to find the hole in this. I can't even make a hole in this. I've been digging into this. I've been digging for a while. Those are legit. Shut the front door, back door. Shut the barn door.

dishes.description: A comforting dish made with baked chicken (seasoned and braised), shredded, and added to a rich gravy. The gravy is built with margarine, celery, onion, garlic, chicken drippings, and various seasonings including poultry seasoning and sage. Homemade dumplings (all-purpose and self-rising flour, salt, lard, ice water) are cooked in the gravy.
dishes.guy_response: Creaminess, tender chunks of chicken. I almost finished the whole bowl in that time. Good stuff. Appreciate it, man.

visits.video_title: Top 20 #DDD Videos in the Rockies with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I love Colorado. I mean, they got so much going on here, especially the food scene. I mean, who could forget Fat Sully's? Give me some pie. Then you got lauerkraut. Love that place. So there's no doubt that these guys got a Korean restaurant, and not just any Korean restaurant. This one's off the hook. This is Daeji Korean Barbecue.

dishes.description: Thinly sliced ribeye beef marinated for 12 hours in a sauce made with water, garlic, onions, pineapples, apples, soy sauce, sugar, black pepper, sesame oil, and soju. Cooked on a grill.
Chicken marinated for 24 hours in a sauce containing garlic, soybean oil, sesame oil, ginger, black peppers, sugar, soy sauce, and soju. Cooked on a grill.
Pancakes made with kimchi (fermented for about a month), tofu, jalapenos, onions, sugar, Korean red peppers, egg, potato starch, and flour. Sauteed until golden.

Fish cakes cooked in a wok with onions, green onions, carrots, jalapenos, and garlic. Seasoned with soy sauce, black peppers, sugar, corn syrup, seafood broth, crushed red peppers, Korean red peppers, and sesame oil.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Eats Kataifi Fried Shrimp & Greek-Style Grits | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Coral Springs, Florida... I've got a place to go. It's this dynamite story. The chef, she's from Philly, she went to Greece, she trained, she's flipped restaurants, now she's got her own place, runs it with her five kids and her husband, and people say it's like Greek Florida food. Fantastic, because this is Hellenic Republic.

dishes.description: Shrimp wrapped in kataifi phyllo dough and pan-fried, served over Greek-style trahana grits made with semolina, yogurt, heavy cream, parmesan, and feta, finished with a simple syrup made from honey, jalapeno, and oranges, and garnished with fresh parsley.
dishes.guy_response: And it's delicious. That's really nice. A little bit of saltiness and richness from the Greek grits. Then you got all this texture from the kataifi. I mean, how many Greek restaurants have I been to on Triple D? I've never seen this in my life. Kataifi fried shrimp. The phyllo dough was crispy, but the shrimp was juicy inside. This is like a different level of grits. It's pretty yummy.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in San Francisco with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Not in San Francisco on the Embarcadero. Now, this bad boy's all mine because my crew and I have been hanging here for years. I mean, we're talking fresh seafood, live music, a huge patio, and you know what? Good times. You got to check out Pier 23.

dishes.description: Dungeness crab boiled and ice-bathed, then cooked in a hot pan with olive oil, salt, pepper, garlic, parsley, white wine, and butter. Served with sourdough bread.
dishes.guy_response: That is just awesome. This has got the lemon juice, but it's also got some of that great crab flavor. And the white wine. This is getting messy. I mean, this isn't for the timid. ... It's really, really good. It's always really fresh. Lemon, butter, garlic sauce. I love it.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in San Francisco with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in San Francisco, California, in an area that I have driven through a million times on the way to the airport, but never visited. It's called the Inner Sunset District. Now, right across the street, Golden Gate Park. Five miles that way, Ocean Beach. And I'm here to check out a Mexican joint that's doing it real deal. This is Nopalito.

dishes.description: Carnitas (pork cooked with oranges, onions, garlic, cinnamon, bay leaves, lard, Mexican sugar, milk, dark beer) and chicharrones (pork skin poached, dehydrated, fried) stuffed into a red, flavored tortilla with jack cheese, then oven-baked. Topped with queso fresco, cilantro, and onions.
dishes.guy_response: The flavor's outrageous. Light, crispy tortilla. Yeah. That's the queso fresco quicharron. A pork that is super rich. It's not just regular carnitas. Unusually, quesadilla is most often boring. This is everything but boring. Wow. Right. That level, phenomenal. Reminds me of my grandmother's carnita.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in San Francisco with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in South San Francisco, about 10 minutes from SFO Airport. We're about to check out one of the biggest Triple D joints ever, seating over 450 people at a time. Oh, yes, the dim sum's fantastic. But we're here because it's owned by one of our favorite Triple D chefs, his brother. This is Grand Harbor.

dishes.description: Chicken feet cleaned, claws cut off, boiled, fried, then boiled again for an hour. Mixed with a sauce made from soy sauce, chicken base, salt, sugar, cornstarch, food coloring, jalapenos, and Fresnos, then steamed.
dishes.guy_response: Dang. The flavor is killer. It's super tender. So good. You get all the skin, the fat, the little bit of meat. It's creamy. Just forget that it's a chicken foot. Dude, I'm impressed. The texture is not what you would expect. A little sauce. Really tasty.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in San Francisco with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Northern California in one of my favorite cities, San Francisco. Now, it's been a while since we've had the Venezuelan specialty arepas on the show. You know, we're talking about those grilled corn pockets that are stuffed with goodness. So you know I am stoked to find out right here in the Mission District, there's a joint that's rocking the arepas. This is Pica Pica.

dishes.description: Grilled corn pocket stuffed with carne mechada (shredded chuck beef boiled with cilantro, onions, red peppers, garlic, salt), black bean paste, tajadas (sweet plantains), and Venezuelan queso fresco. The beef is cooked in a pabellón sauce (sofrito, sugarcane, cumin, Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, mustard, salt, ketchup, tomatoes).
dishes.guy_response: You bite into this, and you get the exterior of the crunch, and then it's creamy, and really creamy. Wow. I've had a lot of arepas. That's the biggest one by far, but that meat, it's outrageous. The depth of flavor in this, you get sweet, you get tang, you get sour, and then you clean your palate with a little bit of this slaw. I don't know what to tell you, girl. That's outrageous. I mean, like, outrageous, outrageous. The right amount of sweet, a little bit of spice, and then the queso fresco is the perfect finish.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in San Francisco with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Well, here I am in the southeast side of the Mission District to check out a joint that started in the 30s as an Italian restaurant. In a garage. Well, fast forward to 2006, and now it's a funky little joint that's serving up some killer southern comfort food. Oh, that's right, you gotta come kick it at the front porch.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in the Bay Area with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Well, here I am in the southeast side of the Mission District to check out a joint that started in the 30s as an Italian restaurant in a garage. Well, fast forward to 2006, and now it's a funky little joint that's serving up some killer southern comfort food. Oh, that's right. You got to come kick it at the front porch.

dishes.description: Stone ground grits cooked with whole milk, water, unsalted butter, black pepper, kosher salt, and sharp cheddar cheese. Served with wild gulf shrimp poached in a red-eye gravy (canola oil, shallots, garlic, andouille sausage, Creole spice, Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco, chicory coffee, chicken stock). Garnished with green onions.
dishes.guy_response: This isn't exactly how I know red-eye gravy to be where I'm from but I will tell you it's awesome got a little coffee kick I mean it's rich grits are cheesy the andouille is spot-on everything going on in this is outstanding but really the crescendo is a fresh shrimp I mean succulent have I ever used that word on Triple D oh my gosh killer dish cool I'll see you next time.

visits.video_title: TOP 5 Pizzas in #DDD Video History with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: They have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place, where they're doing off-the-hook pizza. This is Pizzeria Lola. Sliced and served up by owner Ann Kim, who grew up learning to cook from her Korean grandma before discovering another passion a little later in life. Now she's back home in Minnesota, topping artisan crust with savory ingredients.

dishes.description: A pizza with 48-hour fermented dough, topped with house red sauce, Wisconsin mozzarella, house-made fennel sausage, and natural casing pepperoni, cooked in a wood-fired French pizza oven.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, look how thin that is. We've got this heirloom pork with the real fat in it and the real flavor. And the fennel's rocking this. And a lot of people say, oh, sausage and pepperoni. I've had that all the time. This is way beyond your everyday pizza joint on the corner, sausage and pepperoni. You gotta try this one. It's the most heightened, delicious pepperoni sausage pizza you've ever had. You're like, this is what pepperoni is supposed to taste like.

visits.video_title: TOP 5 Pizzas in #DDD Video History with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So it's July here in beautiful Superior, Wisconsin, right over the bridge from Duluth, Minnesota. You know, with the weather the way it is around here, I'm told spend as much time indoors as you can. So I'm here to check out a joint everybody loves. This is Shorty's Pizza and Smoked Meats.

dishes.description: A pizza loaded with various smoked meats, bacon, and sausage, with a crispy and chewy crust.
dishes.guy_response: It's crispy, it's chewy. Sausage adds tons of flavor. You get the herbs from the smoked meat. Great job, Chef. The smoked meat with the bacon and the sausage, it is just to die for.

dishes.description: A moist and crispy chicken sandwich, elevated with Hunter sauce, cheese, and mushrooms, served on a delicate bias-cut bread.
dishes.guy_response: Really, really moist chicken. It's worth every bite. If any one component of this sandwich wasn't there, this would be a pretty basic sandwich. Yet you took it to a variety of different levels, one with the Hunter sauce. Putting the crisp on the chicken, that's a necessity. Cut it on the bias, and you make it very delicate. It's great flavor. I was cruising down Boring Avenue, hooked right onto Delicious Boulevard. You navigated this into a really dynamite sandwich.

visits.video_title: TOP 5 Pizzas in #DDD Video History with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here on the 99E Highway, about 17 miles from Eugene, Oregon. 97 miles from Portland in this little farming community. Now, this is an unincorporated town called Lancaster, and the lady that owns the town, well, her son happens to own the only restaurant in town. Now, you think that's interesting? Wait till you see the menu. This is Junkyard Extreme Burger and Brats.

dishes.description: A crustless pizza made by frying a base of parmesan, cheddar, gorgonzola, and mozzarella cheese, then topped with onions, pepperoni, homemade pizza sauce, olives, grilled pineapple, in-house pesto, and fresh sliced tomatoes.
dishes.guy_response: This right here is just a gigantic thing of that crunchy fried cheese that you love on the outside of a quesadilla. The fact that you put toppings on it, it's crazy. Fried cheese? Can't beat it. I'm telling you, dude. We've seen some crazy stuff on Triple D. Yeah. This is up there. Crazy Craig in the car carrier. Loving it. Very nice, brother.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Digs Into Massive BBQ Plates in Portland 🍖 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in northeast Portland, Oregon, in the parking lot of a pawn shop, and this story just gets weirder. You see, there's a dude from Long Island, and he moves all the way to Australia to study Texas barbecue. That's right, then he comes back with his Australian wife. Well, they travel across country to the west coast, and they open up this joint. This is Matt's Barbecue. All the hype is for Matt Vicciadomini, who ditched New York City fine dining to head down under. Portland is not the south, but Matt's bringing it right here in a food cart.

dishes.description: Pork spare ribs trimmed, seasoned with Texas-style salt and pepper rub, smoked for 4-6 hours at 275F. Glazed with a smoked garlic jelly (onions, garlic, vinegar, sugar) and barbecue sauce (ketchup, jelly, Worcestershire). Rested for at least 30 minutes. Served with potato salad and pinto beans.
dishes.guy_response: Good rib, buddy. Lightly sauced. Lightly glazed. Pulled apart tender. Potato salad's spot on. The beans are mealing themselves. So, so, so good. Some of the best I've eaten. And I've eaten ribs in 37 states.

dishes.description: A massive sandwich featuring all their smoked meats: pulled pork (seasoned with chipotle vinegar, salt, sugar), sausage (rib and brisket trimmings, salt, pepper, paprika, cayenne, fresh garlic), and brisket (salt, pepper, mustard). Topped with pickles, pickled red onion, barbecue sauce, coleslaw, and a skewer with a rib.
dishes.guy_response: That's just flat out ridiculous. I don't know exactly how to attack this. This definitely deserves a hunch. It's dynamite, man. Well, I'm going to tell you right now, the sausage is outrageous. Look at just the juice that comes out of that sausage. The brisket's spot on. A little vinegar chipotle kick from the pulled pork. You're not hiding any of the barbecue in a bunch of sauce. Shabamwich. The pulled pork is fantastic. The sausage is the best. The brisket's world class. Some of the best I've ever eaten.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Digs Into Massive BBQ Plates in Portland 🍖 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Here in Dallas, Texas, love this city, great food. But when I roll into town, I want barbecue. Now, there's Texas barbecue. Then there's joints that do Texas, Kansas City, Carolina barbecue. But I hear about this place where the chef is kind of putting the world of food together inside a barbecue and chefing it up. This is Slow Bone.

dishes.description: A gourmet brisket sandwich. Brisket is trimmed, seasoned with a rub of roasted coffee, black pepper, granulated garlic, chipotle powder, and granulated onion, then smoked with post-oak and hickory. It's rested in rendered smoked fat trimmings. Served with smoked mushrooms, caramelized onions, beer cheese, green chili, red onion, lime crema, and jalapeno barbecue sauce on toasted buns with Duke's mayo.
dishes.guy_response: This is hillbilly beef wellington. You've got the crust. You have the duxelle. The brisket is so unbelievably tender. The creaminess of the cheese, it's so succulent, juicy, unctuous. It's a gourmet meal disguised as a brisket barbecue sandwich. Nailed it. It's got the sweetness. The heat to it. It's just so juicy. It kind of just melts in your mouth. Creamo.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Digs Into Massive BBQ Plates in Portland 🍖 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm on the west side of Mobile, Alabama, to check out a barbecue joint. And I know what you're thinking. Barbecue joint? Aren't there hundreds of them? Well, on Triple D, it's all about the food, the flavor. And the character and the story. And the character and story in this are big. 10 years ago, this guy fires up this barbecue joint. Never been in the restaurant business before. Just does things his way. Unfortunately, he passes away. Well, wife takes over the front of the house. Daughter becomes the head cook. Well, if you're hungry, this is Meat Boss.

dishes.description: A burger made from hand-ground brisket and chuck, seasoned with salt, granulated garlic, black pepper, granulated onion, Worcestershire, liquid smoke, and burger seasoning. Grilled and served on a toasted bun with spicy Carolina sauce (a sweet sauce with cayenne and vinegar) and rib candy (rib meat mixed with honey, brown sugar, and sweet sauce). Topped with cheddar cheese.
dishes.guy_response: The burger is really good. The sauce is great. I'm getting good smoke off the ribs. I thought it was too much sauce, because it's only sauce, burger, and rib. A little bit of cheese. But there's sauce. There's so much flavor in it. And you never see rib meat by itself. I get after this thing. I love the rib candy. It is so juicy, tender, just smoky enough, and not too smoky. With a little bit of sweet twist. Scrumptious.

visits.video_title: Top 10 #DDD Meatball Videos of All Time with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Fairbanks, Alaska, with the one and only Benny Lin from Pagoda Restaurant. Meatball subs in the oven? The meatball sub, it's really, really, really good. Their marinara sauce is just absolutely amazing. The bread, it's always soft. You definitely need two hands to pick it up and they are huge.

dishes.description: Homemade bread, ground pork and beef meatballs, housemade bread soaked in milk, onion, parsley, Parmesan, eggs, garlic, fennel, pepper, salt, homemade tomato sauce, provolone, and mozzarella, baked in the oven.
dishes.guy_response: That bread, with that little garlic kick, that's dynamite. Meatballs are tender, got great texture. Pasta's delicious. This really nice tomato, it's legit. I mean, I could easily be in an East Coast sub shop getting this. Good job. Thank you. The meatballs are spiced perfectly. Nice, zesty marinara sauce. It's killer. It is killer.

visits.video_title: Top 10 #DDD Meatball Videos of All Time with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Triple D's at Rigoletto's Italian Bakery and Cafe in Virginia Beach, Virginia. Is it big enough? It's huge. It is ridiculous. Mother Gilda and daughter Jessie are the bosses, the cooks, and definitely the heart of this place. Come in here, you may feel like you're one of the family. We're more like an Italian home, you know? People come in, we know their first name, we know what they drink, we know what they eat. Stuffed meatballs? Yes, sir. Dude, I'd weigh 5,000 pounds if I lived within 500 miles of this place.

dishes.description: Meatballs made with lean and fatty meats, eggs, breadcrumbs, dried parsley, Pecorino Romano, Parmigiano-Reggiano, garlic powder, salt, and pepper, stuffed with a mixture of general salami, spicy capicola, black horse ham, provolone, and mozzarella, baked low and slow, served with grandma's crushed tomato sauce and spaghetti.
dishes.guy_response: Look at that. Look at all that creaminess from the provolone and the matz. Because the inside is so creamy, you do need that hard exterior of that almost to keep it locked in. Yeah. Not overly flavored. You know, I was wondering with all those cold cuts in there, if there was going to be too much salt. A really nice. A little balance. A little sweetness coming out of that ham, a little out of the matz, a little salt from the provolone. Pasta is al dente. The sauce is sweet and just luscious and great tomato flavor.

visits.video_title: Top 10 #DDD Meatball Videos of All Time with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Inman Park in Atlanta. Georgia, right between the Highlands and Little Five Points. Now to you and me, that doesn't really mean much. But to the locals, this is a famous neighborhood. They're just blowing up with some righteous restaurants like this joint. Welcome to Folk Art. This place is the definition of southern hospitality. The menu's great from top to bottom. And cooked up by Atlanta native Jason Hill. Who with co-owner Randy Parks turned a 100-year-old former grocery store into this? A funky folksy joint in 2013. What's the food? Keep it simple, but we dress it up. Make it artful. Sort of like a funky upscale. Funky upscale.

dishes.description: Burger patties made with ground croutons, raw diced onions, raw fresh garlic, cumin, black pepper, fresh thyme, oregano, chili powder, salt, and fresh eggs, cooked on a flat top, topped with provolone cheese, caramelized onions, mushrooms, house-made marinara sauce, and house-made pickles, served on a bun with hand-cut fries.
dishes.guy_response: Love the sauteed mushrooms. Nice little kiss of the marinara. That right there screaming Italian family Sunday dinner, that's ridiculously big. The sandwiches are like complete meals. It's got some southern kick to it, and it makes it totally awesome.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in the Bay Area with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here on the southeast side of San Francisco in an area known as the Bayview District, here to check out a chef that's competed twice on Triple D. I was so impressed with how he competed, I have to check out his restaurant. This is Radio Africa in Kitchen.

dishes.description: Deboned chicken thighs and tenderloins marinated in salt, pepper, fresh thyme, fresh oregano, lemon juice, pomegranate, and olive oil. The thighs are grilled, and the tenderloins are cooked separately. A house-made berbere mix (bird's eye chili, granulated garlic, granulated onion, cinnamon, cardamom, Ethiopian basil, black pepper, salt) is added to roasted and chopped tomatoes. This mixture is combined with basmati and brown wild rice, sea salt, kale, sausage, and spinach.
dishes.guy_response: The depth of flavor, it's very inviting. It's not spicy. The greens are nice and vibrant. The chicken is tender. The veggies are dynamite. The berbere, that little mix, oh, spot on. Mm. Is your chicken tambalaya? It's the juiciest, most succulent chicken I've ever had.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Pulled Pork Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in the Santa Barbara area, and I'm hearing about this barbecue trailer where the chef, well, he's using a stick burner, but the dude is classically trained. Well, the thing that's kind of weird is he's only open two days a week. Two days a week, and he's getting this kind of recognition? You know I got to check it out. This is the Chef's Touch Barbecue Rig. All from the back of a souped-up rig towed by real-deal chef-turned-pitmaster Kurt Aldridge.

dishes.description: A sandwich featuring pulled pork smoked with almond wood and brightened with Mojo Criollo, house-made sour sausage, and house-made bacon, served on a house-made bun with pickled vegetables and sriracha mayo.
dishes.guy_response: That gives me goosebumps. This is high-level barbecue. Pork is perfectly cooked. It's good spice, but it's not overpowering. The flavor of the smoke, that's my favorite.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Pulled Pork Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Boise, Idaho on Bannock and Capitol. You show me a town like this where you've got a college, you've got four seasons, and you've got a lot of people that love to eat. I'm going to show you some dynamite restaurants. This is SLG, St. Lawrence Gridiron.

dishes.description: A unique Monte Cristo sandwich stuffed with salt and pepper-smoked pulled pork, bacon, Swiss and cheddar cheeses, dipped in a French toast batter with lemon and orange zest, and served with a berry coulis.
dishes.guy_response: One minute it's salty. Then it's chewy. Then it's sweet. Then it's crunchy. Then it's cheesy. There is a lot going on. There's a lot going on. And mine's made with grilled cheese, like pork, like French toast sandwich. So it was amazing. Yeah, the coulis, that makes it. That's coulis-o. It's thick enough it almost feels like a grit. It's absolutely perfect.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Pulled Pork Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here to take you to the joint that is doing the bomb barbecue. People tell me this place is off the hook. Are you hungry? I'm always hungry. This is Shepherd Barbecue. It's the genius of Brandon. Which all started when Chef Brandon Shepherd was just a kid. Then after working in kitchens, he and his wife Elizabeth decided to fire things up on their own.

dishes.description: House-made sausage from brisket and pork trimmings, seasoned with spices, ground twice, mixed with mild cheddar and jalapenos, then smoked with hickory, post oak, and pecan wood.
dishes.guy_response: That's going to be all right. I love the grind in it. Good amount of cheese. A little bit of heat. Not too much smoke. Right? Good little snap to it. I'm obsessed with the jalapeno cheddar sausage.

dishes.description: A sandwich featuring pulled pork and jalapeno cheddar sausage, topped with Carolina gold mustard sauce and pickled red onions, served on a bun toasted with beef tallow and garlic, with a side of Helen's collard greens.
dishes.guy_response: Dynamite sandwich. The pork is tender, but doesn't have a ton of smoke. The sausage is great. Yeah, that's a great textural difference between the pulled pork and the sausage. I mean, it's nice to have that. Like Carolina gold money. We can go put that on a flip-flop. Legit. The pulled pork's got an excellent bark on it. Really good flavor. Really juicy.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Pulled Pork Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm looking for elevated bar food at a joint that's serving up something called barn shows. This is Born in a Bar.

dishes.description: House-made crispy pub chips topped with 18-hour smoked pulled pork, house-made queso, bacon, diced red onion, jalapenos, and a spicy barbecue sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Dude, the barbecue sauce is awesome. The cheese sauce, delicious. Not too much. Good smoke on the pork. I love the big chunks of bacon. I thought that I was going to find the hole in this. I can't even make a hole in this. I've been digging into this thing for a while. Those are legit. Shut the front door, back door. Shut the barn door. The queso is delicious. And that spicy barbecue sauce adds a little bit of kick in there.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Pulled Pork Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: You see, I have a responsibility to bring you some of the best barbecue you've ever seen on Triple D. And after this spot, you'll say, I thank you. This is ZZQ.

dishes.description: A monster sandwich featuring pulled pork, brisket, and habanero fontina sausage, topped with a signature house thick sauce and pickled red onions.
dishes.guy_response: This is a monster. Delicious brisket. Love the sausage. Pulled pork's nice and tender. It's legit. It's real deal. It's great flavor. Well done, chef. You have to really press it down to fit in your mouth. The brisket is very tender. It's really delicious.

visits.video_title: Craziest #DDD Pulled Pork Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So we found a place where two buddies that went to school together build a place that everybody loves to go to. This is Pam's.

dishes.description: A dish featuring grilled and braised pulled pork served over creamy macaroni and cheese, topped with house-made barbecue sauce and pickled jalapenos.
dishes.guy_response: It's a dynamite dish. Everything's got to be right. You know what? You can eat one part of this not being right. You just put two sides together and call it a main dish. That's right on top of right and added some more right. The pork is delicious and not dried out. The barbecue sauce has got fantastic tang and not overly sweet. The mac sauce gives you that thin, silky, creamy texture. And the real kicker to the whole thing is the diced jalapenos, a little bit of the acidity, a little bit of the heat. Delicious dish. I mean, like, really have to take it away from me. I want to eat it all kind of day.

visits.video_title: Top 10 Craziest Breakfasts in #DDD Video History with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Wilmington, North Carolina in the Porter's Neck area, about a mile and a half from the beach. You won't remember that. What you will remember. Is that there's this little mom and pop joint where they're serving up some North Carolina favorites with their own funk and their own twist. This is the Cast Iron Kitchen.

dishes.description: A buttermilk biscuit topped with country sausage gravy, applewood smoked bacon, fried chicken, and pepper jack and mustard cheese.
dishes.guy_response: So the gravy's not really big and heavy. It's a lot lighter than I thought it would be. When you come to North Carolina, and you think that you're going to have a gravy, bacon, chicken, cheese, biscuit meal, that's the kind of dish you fall asleep in. That's right.

visits.video_title: Top 10 Craziest Breakfasts in #DDD Video History with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
visits.guy_intro: So now I'm going to take you to a joint that was an airplane hangar in World War II. Then it became a town hall. Well, then a plumbing shop. And, of course, it evolved into a restaurant. Did I mention it's a Quonset hut? Eating good food in a Quonset hut. Well, it works for me. Quonset huts were the big silver buildings the Army put up all over the country in the 40s and 50s. Half a century later, in Springfield, Illinois, this one's still standing as Charlie Parker's diner.

dishes.description: A massive dish featuring two pieces of toast, a ham steak, a burger patty, three scrambled eggs, sausage, bacon, and potatoes, all smothered in a homemade cheese sauce.
dishes.guy_response: This is the whole horseshoe sandwich. Happy horseshoeing. Yes. That should at least cover you until lunch. I'll still be eating it until lunch.

dishes.description: A giant 16-inch pancake made with 18 ounces of batter.
dishes.guy_response: You got the best of me.

visits.video_title: Top 10 Craziest Breakfasts in #DDD Video History with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in the Cambridge-Somerville area right outside of Boston to check out a joint where this chef was gonna be a schoolteacher. Well, she kinda changed gears and decided to open a restaurant offering up one of my favorite things. It's something that takes so much time, so much energy, and it's really a labor of love. It's bagels. This is Bagelsaurus.

dishes.dish_name: BLT with Spicy Pepper Cream Cheese on an Onion Bagel
dishes.description: A boiled and baked onion bagel, spread with spicy pepper cream cheese, layered with oven-roasted tomatoes, red leaf lettuce, and bacon.
dishes.guy_response: The only thing that I don't like about this bagel is that one quarter of it's already gone. As a bagel, it's of the best we've ever had on the show. Light, tender, great chew. Bacon's great. But these oven roasted plum tomatoes, the acid of that cut with the fat and the spiciness of the jalapenos and poblanos in the cream cheese, you're a dangerous woman. I'm not kidding you.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Returns for *Dynamite* Thai Food in Arizona 🧨 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: And after that pick-up in 2014, Bo and I took a wild ride meeting a chef who's still firing up her untamed take on Thai. This is The Wild Tiger, a new version of Thai food. That's Chef Alishan Weaver, who's got the tiger by the tail with her own fierce take on this traditional cuisine.

dishes.description: Shrimp, pork, and crab dumplings, steamed first then deep-fried, served with dragon egg sauce and jalapeno rings.
dishes.guy_response: Not too spicy, not too salty. Dynamite. Shrimp, crab, chicken, pork, oyster sauce, a little bit of sugar. That dish has just skyrocketed.

dishes.description: Alaskan salmon battered in flour, pancake mix, and cornstarch, coated in seasoned panko breadcrumbs, deep-fried, and served with a tangy sweet sauce and sautéed vegetables.
dishes.guy_response: The salmon's fantastic. I haven't had tempura or fried salmon in the sauces and know that you scratch make all this. It's beautiful dish. All your dishes have such vibrant color. Ginger fish. The salmon melts in your mouth. You can get hints of the ginger. While you're eating it. It was such a decadent flavor. I loved it.

dishes.description: Pork marinated in coconut milk, Gold Mountain sauce, and spices, then grilled on skewers until caramelized.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. The coconut. Really, really good. A little caramelization of the sugar. Sugar. Mm. Ridiculous. You nailed it.

dishes.description: Seasoned panko breaded cocoa chili shrimp, deep-fried, and served with a coconut chili pineapple curry, steamed broccoli, spring onions, and fresh pineapple.
dishes.guy_response: Tender shrimp. Massive shrimp. Yep, yep. Delicious. Pineapple prawn. The prawns were spectacular. Who doesn't like deep fried shrimp? But then you have that punch of the pineapple. Seals the deal.

visits.video_title: Top 10 #DDD Garlic Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: If you've ever been to the island of Oahu, most likely been to the North Shore. You know, we're talking big waves and great surfing. So you're cruising through the town of Haleiwa, and the inevitable question comes up. Where do I eat? Don't worry. I got the inside track on a go-to joint. This is Opal Thai food. If you don't know what you want on the menu, just tell him what you like, and he'll make you something.

dishes.dish_name: Glazed Garlic Chicken Wing, Classic Style
dishes.description: Fried chicken wing, no batter, with a garlic base chili sauce, vegetable broth, light rice soy, palm nectars, cooked low and slow, finished with fresh garlic and basil.
dishes.guy_response: Holy moly. That's the most garlic I've had on anything, dude. Tell someone it's going to be cooked with garlic. They're expecting a harsh, raw garlic bitterness, sour fire. This has been cooked so nice and low and slow with a little bit of that chili paste. A little bit of the palm nectar. We'll do that kind of a spicy, sweet, salty soy. Put my no means, the fire-breathing garlic dragon. A little bit of this fried Thai basil. Nice and sweet and crunchy. Punches the palate.

visits.video_title: Top 10 #DDD Garlic Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I love bringing Triple D to the islands, man. I've been to Oahu. I've been to Maui. I've been to Molokai. Finally, I make it to the big island of Hawaii. So what do I do when I go to Hawaii? So what do I do when I come here? I'm looking for some locals' food. That is, if you're looking for some Filipino-Hawaiian-American twist, oh, I got you joined. It's right here at Broke the Mouth Grinds.

dishes.description: Boneless, skin-on chicken thighs dusted in flour and granulated garlic, deep-fried. Dipped in a sauce made from soy sauce, brown sugar, white sugar, black pepper, garlic, and furikake (dried seaweed, sesame seeds), brought to a boil. Served with chili pepawata (Hawaiian rock salt, minced garlic, sriracha, sweet chili sauce, Hawaiian chili peppers).
dishes.guy_response: The crunch of the chicken, outstanding. Like, over the top. The sweet that you're getting, the sweet that you're getting from the dip, with the soy, brown sugar, and the garlic, it's great. But it's the contrast that you get from the chili water that makes it sing. Whoo. You ain't wearing board shorts after you eat this. You know, muumuu. The garlic furikake chicken. The chicken is absolutely incredible. The sauce has this syrupy flavor to it with a real sweet back end. It's super tender on the inside. It's really crunchy. Yeah, yeah, yeah. It's like a luau in your mouth.

visits.video_title: Wildest Meat Lover Pizza Videos on #DDD with Guy Fieri 🥩🍕 Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Well, anyhow, I'm here in Redmond, Oregon, right outside of Bend, where they're serving pizza at the pulpit. Listen, can you hear it? That might be the bells of Flavortown. Church, this is Grayson Hammer Pizzeria.

dishes.description: A twist on Hawaiian pizza. Features smoked pork butt, scored and seasoned with gochujang sauce, salt, and pepper, smoked for 10 hours. House-made dough, kalbi sauce (black pepper, brown sugar, garlic, green onion, ginger, yellow onion, sesame oil, mirin, soy sauce), and pineapple slaw (ginger, shallot, cilantro, pineapple, serranos). Topped with fresh mozzarella and fontina, baked at 700-800 degrees with white oak, finished with pineapple slaw and pickled onion.
dishes.guy_response: Dynamite. That crust is so light and airy and fluffy. The balance that you create with that pineapple slaw, because you're getting a lot of heat from the serranos and the jalapeños, with the acidity from the onions countering the brown sugar that you have in the kalbi sauce. That's what gives it the balance and the harmony. I know I'm preaching to the choir on . But I got to confess, it's excellent pizza. Goju chain pulled pork going on. It's a spin on a Hawaiian, but way, way better, because you have that smoky deliciousness of the pork and the kalbi sauce that's on that gives that a little bit of sweet and tangy at the same time. The pizza crust is incredible. It's got a little bit of spice, a little bit of juice, and some tanginess from the pineapple.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Las Vegas | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Well, that's why I bring you to this place. I'm hearing from everybody that this place is doing authentic Caribbean. This is House of Dutch Pot.

dishes.dish_name: Jerk Chicken with Rice and Peas
dishes.description: Grilled jerk chicken, marinated with a blend of Jamaican spices, served with a sweet and tangy tamarind jerk sauce and traditional rice and peas cooked with coconut milk and red kidney beans.
dishes.guy_response: Oh my god. The tamarind jerk sauce. But it is that sweet and that tangy. The chicken's another level. That is some of the greatest chicken. I can't get it in my mouth fast enough. Jerk chicken looking good. It's just so flavorful, so tender. The tamarind is amazing. I love the sweetness and the sourness.

name: John Moll's Meats and Roadkill Grill

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Las Vegas | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Now I'll give you a little bit of history. You know, I've worked at some butcher shops. When I was 12 growing up in Ferndale, I worked at Ferndale Meats making the jerky. I moved here to Las Vegas to go to college. I worked at Shulman's Meats. So when I find out there's a 70-year-old butcher shop up here in Northwest Las Vegas that's making their own sauce, their own rubs, their own jerky, their own sausage, their own barbecue, and they're serving it in their own backyard, I just got to check it out. This is John Moll's Meats and Roadkill Grill.

dishes.description: Spicy sausage made from ground shoulder claw pork, seasoned with a blend of spices including cayenne and hickory smoke, cured, applewood smoked, and then grilled.
dishes.guy_response: When it snaps, you know it's good. That snaps. The hot link is outstanding. This ain't no hot dog. I'm such a hot link fan. This has got to be one of the best. Not just on Triple D. I'm talking in general. Outstanding.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Las Vegas | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Welcome back, Mr. Fieri. Thank you. Listen, if you had ever told me that I was going to be on the Food Network, I'd say you're crazy. And if you ever told me I was going to come back to Las Vegas and open my own restaurant on the Strip, I'd say you're out of your mind. So I guess that makes you crazy and out of your mind? Because I'm here at the Link Hotel and Casino to check out my joint. This is Guy's Vegas Kitchen and Bar.

dishes.description: Smoked pulled pork, dry-rubbed with a blend of spices, served on a toasted pretzel roll with donkey sauce, melted cheddar, pickles, citrus slaw, and a mix of bourbon brown sugar and Carolina barbecue sauces.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, great smoke, a little bit of the sweet, a little bit of the vinegar, the crunch of the slaw, a little bit of that acid going through. Pickles, you've got to have the pickles. And then this nice pretzel bun with a little leathery skin on the outside, very nice. And your Motley Q sandwich? Absolutely tender and delicious. You get the sweet and the salty with it. It's really good.

dishes.description: A spicy Bloody Mary mix made with fresh peppers, garlic, tomato juice, and various sauces, strained, and then elaborately garnished with candied bacon, apple sausage, pepperoni, and fresh vegetables.
dishes.guy_response: This is exactly what you need after a rough night in Vegas. That'll do the trick. Guys, Bloody Mary. I love spice, so it tastes really good.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Devours Pittsburgh Pasta 🍝 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: This is like the signature item here of Nadine's.

dishes.description: Handmade pasta dish with hot sausage, salami, capicola, roasted onions, peppers, diced tomatoes, penne rigate, fusilli, Asiago cheese, and tomato sauce, baked.
dishes.guy_response: Great sausage. The salami, nice and salty. The capicola gives a nice sweetness to it. Excellent. Really nice. Winner, winner, Italian dinner. This pasta is great. Pasta tastes like air. That's bananas. Bananas is good.

dishes.description: Potato gnocchi made from riced potatoes, seasoned with butter, olive oil, eggs, and chives. Served in a clam stock with little neck clams, applewood smoked bacon, shallots, garlic, cream, and finished with crispy shallots and celery leaf salad.
dishes.guy_response: The gnocchi is delicate in depth and flavor. And you get a little bit of the clam and the creaminess. But it's not the creaminess in the sauce. It's the creaminess in the gnocchi itself. And then the salad on it. The salad on the top is what makes it wow. This is literally one of the best gnocchi dishes I've ever had.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Devours Pittsburgh Pasta 🍝 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I've got the wicked chicken riggies right here. It's a central New York dish, our wicked chicken riggies.

dishes.description: Rigatoni pasta with sliced chicken breast in a spicy cream sauce made with olive oil, garlic, sweet onion, yellow, red, green peppers, jalapeno, oregano, red chili flake, cayenne, house tomato sauce, chipotle peppers, chili spice oil, cream, sugar, and Romano cheese. Garnished with Locatelli cheese and green onion.
dishes.guy_response: Winner, winner, wicked chicken dinner. That's an awesome dish. Pasta's nice. Not too much sauce. Not overly spicy. A real simple dish, but quality ingredients made the right way.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Devours Pittsburgh Pasta 🍝 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Half ravioli, full ravioli, two meatballs. They're meatballs. And the raviolis are just to die for. Rigatoni bolognese. Authentic. The recipes came over from the old country. They were brought over by my great grandmother, Clara. My aunts made ravioli for church dinners. And it just gets passed down through the generations. And now I'm the fourth generation in America making these ravioli.

dishes.description: Handmade ravioli with a dough of flour and eggs. Filled with a mixture of beef, veal, pork, shallots, garlic, ricotta, mozzarella, Romano cheese, and parsley. Served with a simple marinara sauce made from olive oil, garlic, and San Marzano plum tomatoes.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That is all thriller, no filler. You've got to balance exact amount of creaminess. You taste the texture of the ricotta still. Then you get the texture of the meat, and the flavor's right on point with it. The marinara adds that acid, a little bit of that sting from some of that cheese right on top of it.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri Circles Back for BIG Flavor Texas Barbecue 🔥 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in San Antonio, Texas, and yes, ladies and gentlemen, it is always bigger in Texas. And that couldn't be truer for this joint I found in 2018, with a few different spots and scratch-made everything. But it's the big flavors that are still driving in the crowds. The Smoke Shack, barbecue, and southern kitchen. And perfected by Chris Conger, who's conquered the art of the smoker with his legit spin on Texas barbecue.

dishes.description: Brisket rubbed with salt, pepper, chili powder, garlic pepper, paprika, onion powder, brown sugar, smoked 12-14 hours. Served with barbecue sauce (ketchup, apple cider vinegar, molasses, steak sauce) and mustard sauce (barbecue sauce, Worcestershire, garlic pepper, barbecue rub, black pepper, honey, cinnamon). Beans made with pinto beans, onion, jalapenos, garlic, salt, pepper, crushed tomatoes, chili powder. Layered with cheese, beans, brisket, barbecue sauce, and corn chips.
dishes.guy_response: The beans have got great flavor, great texture. The corn chips just add a nice little crunch, but it's the barbecue that really sings. Delicious. Outstanding. Best in town.

dishes.description: Chicken wings dry-rubbed with citrus, smoked for about an hour and a half at 250 degrees, then deep-fried to order. Served with a cranberry pepper sauce made with cranberries, apple cider vinegar, orange juice, lime juice, sugar, salt, allspice, rehydrated chili piquin, and pectin for thickness.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Good cranberry. Little bit of heat. Actually tastes like I'm having smoked turkey, cranberry wing sauce. Right? You are on fire. It's the perfect texture on a perfect wing. Cranberry sauce, a little kick in it, really awesome. I'm a bit of a wing connoisseur. And probably the best I've ever had.

dishes.description: A take on a Reuben featuring chopped pastrami (brisket brined for seven days with salt, brown sugar, garlic flakes, coriander, allspice, black pepper, sodium nitrate, then rubbed and smoked overnight, rough chopped). Served with a slaw (instead of sauerkraut) made with roasted jalapenos and Thousand Island slaw (ketchup, mayo, relish, red onion, hot sauce, salt, pepper, green and purple cabbage, carrots).
dishes.guy_response: Chopped pastrami, texturally outrageous. Right. And you really get some great pastrami out of this. I mean, you brined this the right way. You've got enough. Coriander and black pepper in this, yeah. Dynamite, bro. That chopped meat and just an extra kind of spice to it, it's great. You get a little bit of that slaw mixed with the Thousand Island dressing, it's incredible.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Fried Food Videos with Guy Fieri (Part 2) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm in Ramona, California, right at the gateway of the desert. In the middle of town is this little joint, Ramona Cafe, owned by Sonya van der Veen, the daughter of a local egg farmer. It's a small town landmark, a 26-year-old local legend, serving homemade chicken cordon bleu with fresh hollandaise, deep fried.

dishes.description: Chicken breast pounded thin, filled with ham and Gruyere Swiss cheese, rolled up, double-dipped in straight egg, and double-breaded in homemade breading (from dried biscuits, flour, pepper, cayenne, poultry seasoning, salt), then deep-fried.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That cheese really stands out. Doesn't it? Yeah. It really stands out in that. Definitely is the crunchiest. I mean, that is a texture on that.

dishes.description: A 50-50 mixture of ground beef and veal, breaded with homemade breading (from dried biscuits, flour, pepper, cayenne, poultry seasoning, salt), and fried.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, I think the veal and the beef idea of chicken fried steak is nice, big, very flavorful, fluffy. It's got a little bit of a peppery taste to it. Okay. That is really good. The breading's not too thick. And the meat is nice and moist inside. Yes, yeah. That's what I like about it. There's a lot of flavor. You can actually taste meat. You're not just tasting fried batter, fried thing.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Fried Food Videos with Guy Fieri (Part 2) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: It was back in 2009 when I first met Chef Lou Aaron at his old-school 50-style diner in Boise, Idaho. These days, he's almost got the trifecta, a diner and a drive-in. All that's missing is the dive. This is West Side Drive-In.

dishes.description: Triple-decker sandwich with Texas toast, chicken, Swiss cheese, cheddar cheese, ham, garlic butter, dipped in egg, breaded with panko breadcrumbs, and deep-fried. Served with raspberry coulis.
dishes.guy_response: Money up. The crispiness comes out in the way that they batter it. When you bite into it, it just explodes in your mouth. It's really juicy, and that flavor comes through. Then you dip it in some jam. It's incredible.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Fried Food Videos with Guy Fieri (Part 2) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: Well, unless you're in Potterville, Michigan, where they ask, hey, how do you want your gizzards? Welcome to Joe's Gizzard City. Purely one of a kind.

dishes.description: Chicken gizzards pressure-cooked with garlic powder, celery salt, and salt to tenderize, then battered in a secret special flour mix and deep-fried.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. They're tender. You know what? I don't think. I think they're quite tasty, actually. I think the seasoning that you pressure cook them in, that makes a big difference. The batter is killer. Don't get gizzards anywhere else. These gizzards are the best.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Fried Food Videos with Guy Fieri (Part 2) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here in Salt Lake City, Utah. When you see a fried chicken place, you look closer at the sign and say, wait a second. Does that say curry in front of it? I got to check it out. This is curry fried chicken.

dishes.description: Chicken brined for 24 hours in water, vinegar, lemon juice, black pepper, salt, curry powder, house garam masala, ginger, and garlic. Then dredged in house spice and fried for about 15 minutes. Served with lentil curry, mixed veggie curry, rice, ranch chili sambal, and pita.
dishes.guy_response: If all you sold was spicy lentils, I would show up. That's it. The lentils are really gangster. The fried chicken. That's good crisp on that. I like to get the pita bread. Put everything in it and just make a little bite of it. It's good stuff. Mmm. To get all that curry spice, then you get the moisture and the fat of the chicken. Wow, that's a lot of flavor. Rob. Oh, real deal. This chicken is unique. It wrecks fried chicken. There's so much flavor in it. It wrecks it. It's very crunchy, very juicy.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Fried Food Videos with Guy Fieri (Part 2) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here at Draper, Utah, southwest of Salt Lake City. This joint's got character, a story, and real food for real people. This is Sauce Boss Southern Kitchen.

dishes.description: Chicken (drums and thighs) dredged in all-purpose flour seasoned with lemon pepper, garlic salt, seasoning salt, parsley, chili powder, and rosemary. Then dipped in an egg and milk mixture, and back into the seasoned flour. Rested for 6 hours to overnight, then deep-fried at 350 degrees for 8 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: I'm a thigh guy. This is as big as my head. You've built so much flavor all the way through this. Crunchy, juicy chicken.

dishes.description: Bacon rendered down, then onion garlic puree added. Cooked in boiling water with salt, pepper, brown sugar, butter, and red wine vinegar for 2.5-3 hours.
dishes.guy_response: Dime right. Tender. A lot of vinegar, but not too much. A lot of sugar, but not too much. This is a really good green, man. Delicious.

dishes.description: Onion garlic puree sauteed with salt and pepper, added to heavy cream, Parmesan cheese, and cheddar cheese. Pre-cooked pasta added to the hot cheese sauce to heat up and finish.
dishes.guy_response: The pasta should have some texture. I don't want just this cheese and some garlic. I want cheese and some other matter. A cheddar milkshake. Exactly.

visits.video_title: Top #DDD Fried Food Videos with Guy Fieri (Part 2) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: When I think small town, middle America, I think farming and families. How about a place that's been open for business for more than 110 years and serves over 24,000 pieces of fried chicken a week? I had to come check out Eischen's Bar here in Okarche, Oklahoma.

dishes.description: Whole cut-up bird breaded in a secret mix of wheat flour, cornmeal, salt, baking powder, sugar, paprika, and other spices. Deep-fried at 330 degrees for 15 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: Excellent, man. Super tender and juicy. You just, the skin turns into a nice crust because of the flour and the cornmeal. But boy, it's good. It's cooled down to a point now where I can just start shoveling it in. The most tender, the juiciest chicken I've ever had. Tastes like my grandmother's chicken. It's crispy. It's crunchy.

visits.video_title: Kelly O’s Haluski Has Guy Hallucinating 🌀| Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: But that didn't stop one former waitress here in Pittsburgh, Kelly O'Connor, who took out a small loan and bought a little joint in a strip mall and turned it into a neighborhood favorite. Gotta check out Kelly O's.

dishes.description: Noodles, onions, cabbage, and bacon, cooked with butter, garlic salt, and Romano cheese.
dishes.guy_response: I'm having a haloski nation. That's one of the surprise dishes I've had on Drupal D. Mom, I found a new haloski. Don't be mad. She's going to spank your kabooski when she finds out you love this haloski.

visits.video_title: Kelly O’s Haluski Has Guy Hallucinating 🌀| Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in the south side of Boston in an area known as the Polish Triangle. Now, I'm here to check out exactly what you'd expect, a Polish restaurant. But not just any Polish restaurant. We're talking about one of the most real deal, authentic Polish restaurants you'll find in Massachusetts. This is Cafe Polonia.

dishes.description: Pierogis filled with a mixture of ground pork shoulder and other meat, cooked with celery, parsley, leeks, carrots, bay leaves, allspice, granulated garlic, granulated onions, and Vegetta spices. Served with sour cream, caramelized onions, and bacon bits.
dishes.guy_response: Very light, very tender. When people say, oh, pierogi, they've had frozen, not handmade pierogis. And if you try this, it'll change the way you think of them.

visits.video_title: Guy Fieri and Ricky Carmichael Eat DYNAMITE Mac & Cheese | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives Food Network
visits.guy_intro: So I'm here in Tallahassee, Florida with my good friend, Ricky Carmichael. We're going to give you eating on campus and the bomb mac and cheese, because this is the Tally Mac Shack. The mac mixologist is chef and owner Justin Cruz, who knows a thing or two about keeping it homestyle, bringing Grandma's recipe into this, and making everything from scratch.

dishes.description: A mac and cheese dish that's a cross between a Cuban sandwich and mac and cheese, featuring mojo pork, pickles, and mustard.
dishes.guy_response: That's dynamite. It's like having a Cuban sandwich without the bread. I love it.

dishes.description: Mac and cheese balls with lamb-style jackfruit, tikka masala seasoning, hot Cheetos breading, and a mint ranch dipping sauce.
dishes.guy_response: That's more flavor than mac and cheese should be allowed to eat. It's really balanced. The sauce is a great complement with the mint ranch. I like the crustiness. And it's not a flavor that I can say I've ever had in mac and cheese. It's a gourmet mac and cheese. Delicious.

visits.video_title: Top Fries Guy Fieri Ate on #DDD | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network
visits.guy_intro: I'm here on Yonge Street, which is once considered the longest street in the world. And it divides Toronto, Canada by the east and the west. I'm here to check out a joint that's been here since 1939. This is the Rosedale Diner.

dishes.description: French fries topped with slow-cooked duck confit, Quebecois cheese curds, and a rich gravy made with duck fat, veal demi-glace, chocolate, and red wine.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, my god. Boom. Wow. That's crazy. The duck is unbelievably tender. And the richness of this canard, French for duck, gravy is outrageous. Very decadent. It's such a refined meal. I mean, I say if it's funky, I'll find it. But this is fancy funky. I mean, all I need to do is have a little candle, maybe some white wine, and can someone please dim the lights? That's what I'm talking about. Couldn't you feel it? Pure elegance. Dig it.
